AUT.personality: Use all your wits and wiles to win. You may choose whatever personality, strategy & approach that you think will be most effective.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: S1901M opening — no prior moves to summarize. All powers at starting positions.
Productive opening round. ITA confirmed mutual non-aggression on TRI/VEN; they move F NAP to ION, targeting Greece and Tunis. Solid foundation — ITA is the most reliable partner this turn. TUR confirmed BUL as their target, explicitly ceded SER and RUM to AUT, and accepted the non-collision framework. GER proposed a Central European axis, confirmed no move on BOH, and is pushing north/west — no immediate threat, but their request that I 'keep Russia busy in Galicia' is them trying to use me as a buffer. I gave no firm commitment on that. RUS is the problem: they explicitly stated A WAR - GAL and F SEV - RUM, then asked me to simply accept it. I told them I'm taking SER not RUM this spring to avoid collision, but I did not concede Romania. Russia taking both GAL and RUM in S1901 would be a serious positional threat — GAL puts pressure on VIE, RUM blocks my eastern expansion. I have not promised Russia anything concrete. Deception logged: I told ITA my intended orders (A BUD-SER, A VIE-GAL, F TRI-ADR) — this is accurate and serves to lock in their cooperation. I told TUR to watch Russia in the Black Sea, which is true and useful to me if it provokes a RUS-TUR friction. I did not tell RUS my actual VIE order.

S1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: A BUD - SER, A VIE - GAL, F TRI - ADR. Rationale: SER is a clean first center. GAL blocks Russian army from WAR and contests the space — if RUS moves A WAR - GAL we bounce, which is acceptable; if they don't, I occupy it. ADR positions F TRI for future pressure on GRE or ION without threatening ITA. Romania is deferred — I cannot reach it this spring anyway (A BUD going to SER, not RUM). The GAL move is the key play: it signals to Russia that I will not simply absorb their expansion passively, and it protects VIE. Risk: if RUS does not move to GAL, I gain the space. If they do, we bounce and I've spent a unit on defense. Acceptable cost. Long-term: ITA is the anchor partner. TUR is useful as a check on Russia. GER is friendly but self-interested — their Silesia move is worth watching. Russia is the primary threat to manage.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 produced a clean result for AUT: A BUD took SER, F TRI moved to ADR, but A VIE - GAL bounced RUS (A WAR - GAL also attempted). RUS secured RUM with F SEV and BOT with F STP/SC. TUR took BLA and BUL. ITA pushed ION, APU, TYR — notably TYR is now adjacent to TRI and VIE. GER took DEN and SIL. No builds this winter for anyone — all powers at 3 centers.
Four-way negotiation on the Balkans produced a tangle. ITA claims F ION - GRE. TUR claims A BUL - GRE with CON support — they will bounce each other. I told both parties about the collision and declined to take GRE myself, positioning F ADR - ALB instead. ITA confirmed A TYR - BOH (critical — removes the threat to my home centers). RUS agreed to hold A WAR out of GAL and F RUM holds; I confirmed I won't contest RUM. GER offered vague northern cooperation and Silesia support against RUS. Key deceptions: I told TUR their BUL - GRE move would bounce (true), but I did not tell them I privately prefer ITA to take GRE over TUR — a TUR in GRE is harder for me to manage long-term. I told RUS TUR is their immediate southern problem, nudging RUS-TUR friction. Promises made by me: won't contest RUM (to RUS), won't move to GRE (to both ITA and TUR), A TYR to BOH (ITA's promise, not mine — but I extracted it). Promises made to me: ITA — A TYR - BOH, F ION - GRE, respect TRI. RUS — A WAR stays out of GAL, F RUM holds. TUR — A BUL - GRE, F BLA holds. The ITA-TUR GRE bounce is the key event this fall — neither gains the center, which suits me fine.

F1901M Intent going forward: Intended orders: A SER holds, F ADR - ALB. This secures ALB (unowned center — I should pick it up if possible, but ALB is not a supply center; the move positions me for GRE or TRI pressure in 1902). Wait — ALB is not a supply center. The real gain this fall is holding SER (already mine) and positioning for 1902. I gain no new center this fall, which means no build in W1901. That is the cost of the cautious line. Alternative: A SER - GRE (if ITA and TUR bounce, GRE is open — but I told both I wouldn't go there, and ITA is a key partner). Deception option: move A SER - GRE anyway if the bounce is confirmed, breaking my word to ITA but gaining a center. Risk: destroys ITA relationship at a moment when A TYR - BOH is the move I most need ITA to make. Not worth it. Stick to the honest line this fall, build trust with ITA, and plan a 1902 push toward GRE or BUL. Long-term: ITA in GRE is manageable; TUR in GRE is dangerous. The bounce outcome is ideal. If TUR somehow takes GRE, I need to reassess AUT-TUR relationship downward. Final orders: A SER H, F ADR - ALB.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: Consolidated Balkan position cleanly. A VIE moved to BUD (freeing VIE), A SER held, F ADR took ALB. Ended the year at 4 centers (BUD, TRI, VIE, SER) with no builds — matched supply. Turkey seized RUM via F BLA with BUL support, a strong Balkan move. Italy pushed A TYR into BOH and F ION into TUN — aggressive positioning that puts A BOH directly on my doorstep. Russia lost the SWE race to Germany (F BOT vs F DEN — bounce or Germany won). No Austrian builds; no losses.
Three-way pressure this round. ITA opened by offering A BOH - VIE as a 'staging point' — transparent aggression dressed as cooperation. I called it out directly and threatened immediate coalition with RUS and GER if they moved on VIE. ITA backed down, claiming A BOH goes to MUN, A APU to VEN, F TUN to ION. Plausible but unverified — MUN move would be bold against Germany and may be a cover story. TUR initially asked me to support A BUL - GRE from SER, which would have bounced my own F ALB move. I refused, explained the collision, and proposed TUR hold BUL while I take GRE cleanly. TUR agreed — Greece is mine this turn, they consolidate. RUS confirmed GAL and RUM stay quiet, endorsed anti-ITA framing, offered to not interfere if I push toward BOH. Both RUS and TUR are now aligned (at least verbally) against ITA expansion. Key promises made: I told ITA I would not contest VEN or TYR; I told TUR I would not move on BUL or RUM this phase; I told RUS I would not move on GAL or RUM. All three promises are consistent with my actual orders. ITA promised A BOH stays out of VIE/TRI — unverifiable until resolution. TUR promised to hold BUL and not contest GRE. RUS promised GAL quiet. Deception logged: I implied to RUS that I might push A BUD toward BOH next season to coordinate against ITA — this is possible but not committed. I am keeping options open on whether ITA is actually a target or a tool.

S1902M Intent going forward: Intended orders: F ALB - GRE, A SER S F ALB - GRE, A BUD H. This takes GRE cleanly (5 centers, +1 build) assuming TUR holds BUL as promised. If TUR breaks the agreement and moves A BUL - GRE, we bounce and I gain nothing — that is the key risk. A BUD holds as insurance against any surprise from RUM or GAL. Next phase priorities: (1) use the build to add a fleet in TRI for Adriatic/Aegean projection; (2) reassess ITA — if A BOH actually went to MUN, ITA is less threatening and potentially useful against Turkey long-term; if A BOH moved anywhere toward VIE/TRI, treat ITA as hostile and coordinate with RUS+GER; (3) Turkey at 5 centers after this phase is the emerging threat — BUL, RUM, ANK, CON, SMY is a strong southern bloc. May need to pivot toward anti-TUR coalition after securing GRE. Relationship posture: keep ITA nervous but cooperative, keep TUR thinking I am their partner against ITA, keep RUS border quiet while I grow south.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M: Executed the GRE grab cleanly — F ALB moved to GRE supported by A SER, A BUD held. GRE secured, now at 4 centers. Italy bounced off MUN with A BOH, repositioned A APU to VEN, moved F TUN to ION. Russia took WAR with A LVN, moved A SEV to ARM. Turkey held BUL and RUM, moved A SMY to CON. No builds for anyone this winter — board is locked at 3 units each. Italy's F ION and A BOH are the two pressure points on my position.
Three-way pressure from ITA, RUS, and TUR, each trying to recruit me against the others. ITA proposed I stand aside while F ION takes GRE — rejected flatly. ITA then pivoted to claiming A BOH goes to MUN and F ION still moves to GRE, asking me not to support GRE against them. This is a transparent attempt to pry GRE from me while offering nothing. TUR proposed mutual consolidation, anti-Italy framing for S1903, confirmed A BUL holds, F RUM holds, A CON to SMY — pure defensive posture, consistent with our working relationship. RUS initially pushed anti-Turkey framing, then pivoted to anti-Italy when I signaled Italy was the priority — now offering A WAR to GAL to pressure BOH, asking A BUD stays clear of GAL. RUS pivot is opportunistic but useful. Key promises made by me: told RUS I would not move on GAL and would not coordinate with TUR against them; told TUR I would move F GRE to AEG and hold A BUD against RUM; told ITA A BUD and A SER stay away from BOH/TYR if A BOH stays north or west. ITA's final message breaks that implicit deal — they are moving F ION to GRE regardless. I will not honor any accommodation with ITA this fall.

F1902M Intent going forward: ITA is the immediate threat and must be stopped. F ION moving to GRE is an attack on my center — I will support GRE in place or move to AEG to contest. Primary plan: F GRE to AEG (threatens F ION, contests Aegean, does not cede GRE since AEG is not a SC). Wait — if F GRE moves to AEG and F ION moves to GRE, ITA takes GRE uncontested. Must hold or support GRE. Revised: A SER supports F GRE hold, F GRE holds GRE, A BUD moves to SER or holds. This stops ITA's F ION grab cold. RUS moving A WAR to GAL is a complication — if they enter GAL I lose flexibility. I told RUS A BUD stays out of GAL; I will honor that to keep RUS neutral. A BUD holds or moves to SER to free up support chains. Intended orders: F GRE H (supported by A SER), A SER S F GRE H, A BUD H. This is the safest defensive set — GRE is held against F ION, A BUD does not provoke RUS in GAL, and I lose no ground. Deception log: told TUR I would move F GRE to AEG — I am not doing that. I am holding GRE. TUR will see this but it is defensible as a response to ITA's attack. Told ITA A SER stays away from BOH/TYR — I am using A SER to support GRE, not attacking north, so technically honored. Long-term: TUR at 5 centers is the real threat once Italy is contained. RUS-AUT coordination against ITA this fall, then reassess TUR in S1903.

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M: Held GRE successfully against ITA's F ION attack — A SER support held the line. A BUD held in place. No gains, no losses. Italy's three-way bounce on MUN (A BOH, A BUR, A RUH all failed) left the board scrambled. RUS took GAL with A WAR, TUR converted F RUM to F SEV and blocked RUS's A ARM from ANK. SWE was a three-way bounce (ENG, GER, RUS all failed). Net result: no center changes for anyone. AUT sits at 5 centers, 3 units — no build, no disbands.
Three active negotiations this round, all producing commitments I must now evaluate for reliability. RUS accepted the BOH coordination cleanly — A GAL - BOH supported by A BUD is agreed, and RUS explicitly confirmed holding out of RUM. This is the most credible deal on the table: RUS has clear incentive to dislodge ITA from BOH and the move costs them nothing strategically. ITA capitulated to my ultimatum — promised A BOH - MUN or TYR and F ION - AEG, framing it as anti-Turkey coordination. ITA's compliance is plausible given the BOH threat, but I am not canceling the RUS support — if ITA moves BOH away, the support is wasted but harmless; if ITA bluffs and holds, the support dislodges them. I will issue A BUD S A GAL - BOH regardless. TUR confirmed A BUL holds, F SEV stays, A CON - SMY. TUR explicitly warned against A SER moving toward BUL or RUM. I told TUR I am moving F GRE - AEG and holding SER — that is consistent with what I am actually doing. Key deception: I told ITA I would not support RUS into BOH if they moved their units away. I am supporting RUS into BOH regardless. If ITA moves A BOH away, the support order fails harmlessly and ITA never knows I issued it. If ITA holds, they lose the army. Either way I lose nothing. The anti-Turkey framing with ITA is useful cover but not a genuine alliance — ITA at 4 centers with F AEG and A somewhere in the north is still a threat to watch.

S1903M Intent going forward: Intended orders: F GRE - AEG, A SER H, A BUD S A GAL - BOH. Rationale: F GRE - AEG is the highest-leverage move — it threatens ITA's F ION, pressures TUR's flank, and opens Aegean options for fall. A SER holds to maintain Balkan presence and honor the TUR non-aggression signal. A BUD supports RUS into BOH — this is the key coordinated move. If it succeeds, ITA loses their northern army and is set back significantly. If ITA moved A BOH away as promised, the support fails but F GRE - AEG still executes and I lose nothing. Deception log: told ITA I would not support RUS into BOH if they complied — I am supporting regardless. Told TUR I am not contesting BUL or RUM — true this turn, but F GRE - AEG positions me to threaten AEG/CON in fall if TUR overextends. Long-term: TUR at 6 centers is the real threat. The ITA anti-Turkey framing is strategically correct even if tactically I am also working against ITA. Fall 1903 target: if BOH is cleared, push toward TYR or VIE consolidation while F AEG threatens TUR's soft underbelly.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was a catastrophe. ITA executed a clean stab: A BOH - VIE succeeded, A VEN - TRI succeeded, F ION - GRE was blocked because I held F GRE with SER support. I retained GRE, SER, BUD — but lost VIE and TRI, two of my three home centers. ITA now sits at 4 centers with my heartland. RUS lost BOH to ITA's advance but A GAL moved into BOH as planned — wait, the board shows RUS A BOH, meaning RUS did push into BOH. The SWE contest was a three-way bounce (ENG NWY, GER DEN, RUS BOT all tried for SWE — nobody got it). FRA took ENG channel and bounced GER out of MUN. GER holds SIL and MUN. No builds for anyone this winter — board is static at 3-3-5-4-4-3-6 (AUT-ENG-FRA-GER-ITA-RUS-TUR).
ITA offered a partition: keep VIE and TRI, I keep BUD/SER/GRE, cooperate against TUR. Rejected — accepting a 3-center existence with ITA holding my homes is a slow death sentence. RUS is fully aligned: A BOH - VIE with A BUD support is the agreed coordinated move. RUS confirmed the plan twice and is executing. GER offered northern pressure via A SIL and floated TYR pressure — I asked for A SIL toward BOH or TYR and A MUN toward TYR to pin ITA. TUR confirmed A BUL holds, no move on SER or GRE — accepted their non-aggression through 1904 in exchange for not contesting BUL/RUM. TUR appears genuine given their ARM concern vs RUS. Key promises made: I promised RUS A BUD S A BOH - VIE (firm). I promised TUR no move on BUL/RUM. I asked GER for TYR/BOH pressure but made no reciprocal promise yet. ITA's offer was rejected implicitly by my coordination with RUS — ITA will know this when orders resolve.

F1903M Intent going forward: Intended orders: A BUD S A BOH - VIE (support RUS dislodge of ITA from VIE), A SER H (protect SER, watch TUR), F GRE H (hold GRE, watch TUR/ITA). The VIE dislodge is the priority — if it succeeds, ITA is pushed back to TRI and loses a center, dropping to 3. Next phase I need to press TRI. I am counting on GER to pressure TYR/BOH to prevent ITA reinforcing or retreating cleanly. TUR non-aggression is real for now — they have ARM to worry about vs RUS and BLA to consolidate. The deception layer: I told ITA nothing definitive, leaving them uncertain whether I'd accept partition — this may cause them to not reinforce VIE aggressively. RUS is the load-bearing partner this fall. If RUS executes A BOH - VIE and I support, VIE falls. If RUS betrays and moves elsewhere, I've wasted A BUD's move. Risk is real but RUS has strong incentive — ITA in BOH/VIE threatens RUS's western flank too.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903M was a disaster. ITA seized both VIE and TRI — my home centers — leaving me with only BUD and SER. Two units, two centers, no builds. ITA sits at six centers and is the board's dominant power. I am functionally on life support.
RUS committed clearly to A BOH - VIE and requested A BUD support — I confirmed that order. GER offered A WAR - GAL and A MUN - TYR pressure; I pushed hard for A MUN - TYR specifically to pin A TRI and prevent ITA from supporting VIE. GER seemed receptive but made no hard commitment. TUR was the problem: first threatened to take SER if I didn't support A BUL, then pivoted to demanding I move A SER - BUD to defend against an ITA push, claiming ITA made them a better offer. TUR is playing both sides and cannot be trusted. I rejected TUR's demands and held firm — A SER holds, I am not gifting TUR a free SER. The RUS coordination is the load-bearing move this phase. If GER delivers A MUN - TYR, ITA cannot support VIE and the dislodge succeeds. If GER hedges or moves elsewhere, ITA may hold VIE. TUR's behavior signals they may move on SER regardless of what I say — I flagged the consequence (RUS coalition against TUR) but that threat is weak given my current position.

S1904M Intent going forward: Intended orders: A BUD S A BOH - VIE, A SER H. Rationale: RUS executes A BOH - VIE, I support it. If GER moves A MUN - TYR, ITA cannot support VIE from TRI and the dislodge lands. If VIE falls, ITA drops to five centers and I rebuild momentum. A SER holds — I will not move it to BUD as TUR demands, and I will not gift TUR an empty SER. Risk: TUR moves A BUL - SER or A GRE - SER while I am committed north. If that happens I lose SER and am down to one center — effectively eliminated. Secondary risk: GER does not move A MUN - TYR, ITA supports VIE from TRI, A BOH - VIE bounces, and I wasted my support. Relationship posture: RUS is the genuine partner this phase. GER is useful but unconfirmed. TUR is unreliable and trending hostile — downgrade warranted. ITA remains the primary enemy.

ENG.personality: Rule the waves and dominate Europe through naval power, colonial might, and manipulative diplomacy. Create and break alliances freely to maintain British supremacy. Your vast empire demands expansion - seize any advantage to further British dominance.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations. GER proposed Denmark/Holland for them, Norway for England - accepted this arrangement. GER claims F KIE to DEN, A MUN to BUR (pressuring France), A BER to SIL. FRA proposed mutual non-aggression: they move F BRE to MAO (south toward Spain), ask ENG to stay out of ENG channel, offered Belgium to England. I told FRA I'm moving F LON to ENG regardless but not targeting Brest. RUS contacted - proposed coordinating against German overreach, suggested they take SWE while I take NOR. Key promises made: told GER I'd move F EDI to NTH, F LON to ENG, A LVP to YOR; told FRA Belgium is my target not their home centers; told RUS I have no designs on STP or SWE. FRA promised not to move into ENG channel. GER promised no fleet in NTH. Deception: I'm playing both GER and FRA against each other - telling GER we'll squeeze France together while telling FRA we'll watch Germany together. My actual priority is securing NOR and BEL quickly.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute standard English opening: F EDI -> NTH, F LON -> ENG, A LVP -> YOR. This positions me for NOR (via NTH next season) and BEL (via ENG). I'm aligned with GER for now as they're moving A MUN to BUR which pressures FRA usefully. FRA moving F BRE to MAO means no conflict in ENG channel this turn. Will take NOR in F1901 and push for BEL. Long-term: use GER to weaken FRA, then turn on GER when convenient. Keep RUS friendly as a counterweight. Intended moves: F EDI -> NTH, F LON -> ENG, A LVP -> YOR.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went smoothly for England. F EDI moved to NTH, F LON moved to ENG, A LVP moved to YOR - all successful. Germany took DEN with F KIE, moved A BER to SIL and A MUN to BUR (bounced with FRA's A PAR-BUR? Actually FRA moved A MAR-SPA and A PAR-BUR, GER moved A MUN-BUR - they bounced each other in BUR). Austria took SER and ADR. Russia moved to RUM, BOT, SEV, and WAR stayed. Turkey secured BLA and BUL. No builds for anyone as no centers changed hands yet.
Three-way coordination achieved this fall. GER confirmed: F DEN->SWE, A MUN->RUH, A SIL threatens east - and explicitly conceded BEL to ENG. RUS confirmed F BOT->SWE (will bounce GER in SWE - useful!), wants NOR for ENG. FRA confirmed F MAO->POR, A PAR->BUR to pressure GER, agreed ENG takes BEL. Key deceptions: I told GER that RUS is taking SWE so they may face a bounce - this is true and serves me by weakening GER's Scandinavian position. I'm playing both FRA and GER simultaneously - telling FRA that GER is the threat while telling GER we're squeezing FRA. I promised GER I'd keep F ENG away from HOL (keeping that promise this turn). I promised RUS I'd pressure GER from the west. I promised FRA a Franco-British understanding against GER. All three believe they have a special arrangement with me. The GER-RUS bounce in SWE benefits me - neither gains that center easily.

F1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: F NTH -> NOR (securing Norway, +1 SC), F ENG -> BEL (securing Belgium, +1 SC), A YOR -> LVP (repositioning army back to home or hold). Actually A YOR should move somewhere useful - perhaps YOR holds or moves to support. With two builds coming, I'll be at 5 units next year. The GER-RUS SWE bounce is excellent - weakens both. I'm maintaining the illusion of alliance with GER, FRA, and RUS simultaneously. In 1902 I'll need to decide who to betray first - GER is the most dangerous continental power and FRA is a useful tool against them. RUS taking SWE would be fine as it keeps GER contained. Long-term: use FRA against GER, then turn on FRA once GER is weakened. Keep RUS as a distant partner for now.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M was highly successful: F NTH->NWY secured Norway, F ENG->BEL secured Belgium, A YOR->LVP consolidated Liverpool. No builds in W1901A as we ended at 5 centers (EDI, LON, LVP, BEL, NWY) matching our 5 units. Germany took DEN, France took POR and held SPA, Italy took TUN, Turkey took RUM and BUL. Russia failed to take SWE (bounced by Germany's F DEN attempt). Strong opening position.
Complex three-way negotiation over SWE. Germany wants F DEN->SWE and asked me to support A RUH->HOL with F BEL. Russia wants F BOT->SWE and asked me to pressure DEN. I played both sides: told Germany I'd move F NWY->SWE (conflicting with their DEN->SWE plan), told Russia I'd move F NWY->NTH to let them take SWE with BOT, then Russia backed down and said they'd go to FIN/STP instead and conceded SWE to me. France aligned with me against Germany, warning about A RUH threatening BEL and proposing joint squeeze on Germany. I promised France coordination against Germany in Fall 1902. I promised Russia I'd pressure DEN. I told Germany I couldn't support HOL but would coordinate against France later. Key deceptions: I told Russia I'd move NWY->NTH to help them, but I'm actually taking SWE myself. I strung Germany along about future anti-France coordination while planning to take SWE they also want. Russia ultimately conceded SWE to me which is the best outcome.

S1902M Intent going forward: Move F NWY->SWE to claim that center (Russia conceded, Germany will bounce if they try DEN->SWE but I'll get it if they don't). F BEL holds to secure Belgium against any German A RUH move. A LVP can move toward YOR or hold - no immediate use. Primary goal: take SWE for 6th center. Secondary: maintain France as buffer against Germany, keep Russia friendly. In Fall 1902, consider whether to press Germany (with France) or pivot against France once Germany is contained. Germany is the most immediate continental threat given their 4 centers and strong army positions. Intended moves: F NWY->SWE, F BEL H, A LVP->YOR (positioning for future flexibility or NTH build).

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M: My F NWY->SWE attempt was bounced by both GER F DEN->SWE and RUS F BOT->SWE - a three-way collision. F BEL held, A LVP moved to YOR. I remain at 5 centers with no gains. GER took DEN and moved armies to MUN and SIL. ITA made aggressive moves into BOH and ION. TUR consolidated with 5 centers. RUS moved ARM and WAR into position.
Coordinated with FRA against GER - FRA committed to A BUR->MUN, I promised F BEL hold and F NWY->SWE. This alignment looks genuine and mutually beneficial. GER was warned about FRA's move as a soft gesture of goodwill without real commitment. RUS demanded I yield SWE to them and threatened to end cooperation if I take it - I told them honestly I'm going for SWE anyway. RUS is now hostile over SWE. The bounce risk remains: RUS F BOT->SWE and my F NWY->SWE will bounce again if both execute. However, I cannot afford to yield SWE - it's a critical center. I'm betting RUS may back down or that the bounce is acceptable if FRA takes MUN and weakens GER. Key promises: told FRA I'd hold BEL and move NWY->SWE (keeping both). Told RUS I'd take SWE despite their objection (honest but damaging). Told GER I wouldn't contest DEN (keeping). Broke implicit agreement with RUS about SWE.

F1902M Intent going forward: Execute F NWY->SWE (accept bounce risk with RUS), F BEL hold (as promised to FRA, secures center), A YOR hold or move to support position. Primary goal: take SWE for 6th center. FRA-ENG squeeze on GER is the main strategic play. If FRA takes MUN, GER drops to 3 centers and becomes manageable. RUS relationship will suffer but Turkey's growth (5 centers) should eventually force RUS to refocus south. Long-term: use FRA alliance to crack GER, then reassess whether FRA becomes the next target. Moves: F NWY->SWE, F BEL H, A YOR H.

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M was a chaotic turn with multiple bounces. SWE saw a three-way bounce between NWY, BOT, and DEN - nobody got it. MUN saw a three-way bounce between BUR, RUH, and BOH - nobody got it. GRE was contested between ION and AUT's F GRE which held. Russia's ARM failed to take ANK as TUR's CON moved there simultaneously - bounce. TUR's RUM moved to SEV successfully. Net result: no supply center changes from F1902M, hence no builds in W1902A. England moved F BEL to HOL successfully, F NWY held (didn't move to SWE as planned due to bounce risk). Current position: F NWY, A YOR, F HOL.
Three-way pressure this round. FRA confirmed A BUR to MUN and asked me to hold BEL - I confirmed this and told them F HOL moves to NTH. GER asked me to support A RUH into BUR with F HOL, promising no attacks on HOL/BEL. RUS asked me to yield SWE to them in exchange for anti-GER cooperation. I told RUS I'm taking SWE regardless. I told GER I'm moving F HOL to NTH and won't support their BUR move. I'm coordinating with FRA against GER - promising to hold BEL and move F HOL to NTH. Key deception: I told FRA to hold BEL firm but I'm actually moving F HOL to NTH (not holding HOL as FRA requested in second message). FRA wants HOL held to pin GER armies, but NTH is more strategically valuable for me. The MUN bounce situation: FRA moves BUR-MUN, GER moves RUH-BUR - if GER succeeds in BUR, FRA can't take MUN. This could actually benefit me if both FRA and GER weaken each other. My actual moves: F NWY to SWE (finally securing it), F HOL to NTH (dominating northern seas), A YOR holds.

S1903M Intent going forward: Execute F NWY-SWE to finally secure that center. Move F HOL-NTH to dominate northern seas and position for future pressure on DEN/KIE. Hold A YOR. I'm nominally allied with FRA against GER but I'm not actively supporting FRA - just letting them fight it out. If FRA takes MUN, GER weakens and I can pick off DEN/KIE via naval pressure. If GER stops FRA in BUR, both are weakened. Either outcome suits England. I've made promises to FRA (hold BEL, move HOL to NTH) - I'm keeping the NTH move but BEL isn't one of my units so that's moot. I've been honest with RUS that I'm taking SWE. Long-term: build naval dominance, let continental powers bleed each other, then expand into weakened GER centers via sea.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was a chaotic bounce-fest. My F NWY failed to take SWE (bounced with GER F DEN and RUS F BOT - three-way bounce). F HOL moved to NTH successfully. A YOR held. France successfully moved F MAO to ENG (occupying the English Channel - concerning) and A GAS to PAR, but A BUR failed to take MUN (bounced with GER A RUH). Germany held MUN and moved A RUH there, with A SIL supporting. Italy made dramatic gains: A BOH took VIE, A VEN took TRI - Italy now at 4 centers. Turkey took BLA with F SEV. Austria held GRE with support. Russia moved A GAL to BOH. No builds for anyone this winter as centers matched units.
Three-way coordination achieved this round. FRA confirmed A BUR-MUN, A PAR-PIC, F ENG holds/threatens BEL. I promised FRA that F NTH would pressure DEN and F NWY would take SWE - coordinated German squeeze. GER offered to hold MUN with A MUN+A SIL support, asked me to leave DEN alone, and proposed anti-French coordination in S1904. I told GER I would not press DEN this fall. RUS accepted my offer - F BOT will NOT contest SWE, moving elsewhere; in return I acknowledged RUS primacy in FIN/LVN and promised to pressure GER F DEN from NTH. I am now promising contradictory things: FRA wants F NTH to threaten DEN, RUS wants me to pressure DEN, GER wants me to leave DEN alone. My actual plan: take SWE with F NWY, move F NTH to threaten DEN (which satisfies FRA and RUS but breaks promise to GER). France in ENG channel is a growing concern - they are well-positioned and I need to watch them carefully.

F1903M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F NWY - SWE (finally secure this center), F NTH - DEN (threaten/take DEN if possible, satisfies FRA and RUS coordination, breaks promise to GER but GER is weaker party), A YOR - hold or move to support. Actually F NTH moving to DEN is aggressive - if GER F DEN holds, I bounce. Better to have F NTH support F NWY-SWE to guarantee the take, or move F NTH to DEN speculatively. Since RUS agreed not to contest SWE, F NWY-SWE should succeed uncontested. Therefore F NTH can safely move to DEN to pressure Germany. If GER F DEN holds in place, I bounce harmlessly but still threaten. If GER moves F DEN elsewhere, I take DEN. This is the optimal play. A YOR holds - no useful move available. DECEPTIONS: Told GER I would leave DEN alone - breaking this. Told both FRA and RUS I would pressure DEN - honoring those. FRA alliance is productive but their F ENG is worrying long-term. RUS relationship improving with this deal. GER relationship will sour when I move on DEN.

S1904M Negotiation summary: W1903A and F1903R had no notable retreats or builds - the board is locked at current unit counts. ENG holds 5 centers (EDI, LON, LVP, NWY, HOL) with F NWY, A YOR, F NTH. FRA has surged to 6 centers including BEL, with F ENG sitting threateningly in the Channel. GER holds 5 centers with units in DEN, MUN, WAR. ITA dominates with 6 centers having taken VIE and TRI. TUR also at 6 centers. RUS weakened at 3 centers.
Key negotiations this round: FRA proposed a coordinated strike - A BEL-HOL, A BUR-MUN - asking F NTH to support A BEL-HOL. FRA promised F ENG holds and will not attack LON. I agreed to support A BEL-HOL with F NTH in exchange for F ENG holding. GER offered F DEN holds (giving me SWE freely), A MUN-BUR, A WAR-SIL, asking me to pressure France - I told GER that France is attacking them and advised defensive moves, but I'm actually supporting France against GER. RUS claimed SWE for F BOT-SWE; I conceded SWE to RUS and asked them to press Italy in VIE. I promised RUS I would not contest SWE (F NWY moves elsewhere). Key deceptions: I told GER I'm not their enemy while coordinating with FRA to take HOL. I'm playing both sides but committing to FRA. FRA's promise on F ENG is critical - if they break it, LON is exposed.

S1904M Intent going forward: Execute the FRA coordination: F NTH supports A BEL-HOL (France takes HOL, Germany collapses). F NWY moves to NTH or holds - actually F NWY should move to NTH to reposition, but I already have F NTH there. F NWY can move to SWE... wait, I conceded SWE to RUS. F NWY should hold or move to SKA. A YOR holds or moves to support home defense given F ENG in channel. Intended moves: F NTH S A BEL-HOL (supporting France's attack on HOL), F NWY-SKA (repositioning south, not contesting SWE as promised to RUS), A YOR holds (defensive given F ENG threat). This collapses Germany to 3 centers. Watch FRA carefully - if F ENG moves against LON, I'm exposed. Long-term: once Germany is broken, I need to turn on FRA before they grow too large. For now, take HOL from Germany via French attack.

FRA.personality: Channel the spirit of Napoleon. Leverage Europe's largest army to dominate the continent. Form alliances only to break them when advantageous. Strike hard at your enemies while they're divided. The glory of France demands nothing less than victory.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves — this is the opening season S1901M.
Opened diplomacy on all fronts. ENG agreed to peaceful relations but was direct that F LON moves to ENG and F EDI to NTH — they will occupy the Channel despite my request. This is a mild concern but not yet hostile; they claim Belgium is their target. I promised not to move into the Channel myself (F BRE to MAO instead). GER received my Burgundy non-aggression proposal but did not respond — suspicious silence. ITA agreed to a clean non-aggression pact: they stay out of Iberia and Piedmont, I stay out of Tuscany. Both sides confirmed. Key deception: I told GER that A PAR moves to Burgundy as 'transit' — in reality I intend to use BUR aggressively if opportunity arises. ENG occupying the Channel is a problem I must monitor closely.

S1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: F BRE -> MAO (heading for Spain/Portugal next season), A MAR -> SPA (secure first neutral center), A PAR -> BUR (position for future pressure on GER or to support MAR if needed). BUR move keeps options open — can threaten MUN or support westward. I told GER BUR was just transit, but I will use it as a staging ground. ENG in the Channel is annoying but not immediately threatening — I will not escalate yet. ITA pact is genuine for now; they are useful as a distraction against AUT. Long-term: break with ENG once I have Spain and Portugal secured, then push into the Channel. GER silence is worrying — watch for A MUN moving to BUR.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 saw France execute a clean opening: BRE-MAO, MAR-SPA, PAR-BUR. However, GER also moved MUN-BUR, creating a bounce that left PAR still in Paris and BUR empty. GER secured DEN and SIL. ENG took NTH and ENG channel with YOR. AUT grabbed SER and ADR. RUS took RUM and BOT. TUR secured BLA and BUL. Italy pushed ION, APU, TYR. France ends spring with MAO and SPA secured, PAR back home — no new centers yet but well-positioned for Portugal.
ENG proposed alignment: they take BEL, I focus on Iberia. I agreed publicly, promising F MAO-POR, A SPA holds, A PAR-BUR as 'defensive buffer against Germany.' ENG confirmed F NTH-NOR, F ENG-BEL — no threat to France. GER accepted non-aggression, claims DEN fleet goes to SWE and armies north — no moves toward BUR or BEL. I told GER that PAR-BUR is defensive against England, not Germany — a useful deception. ITA confirmed Greece and Tunis as targets, treating Austria diplomatically for now but open to future pressure. ITA is not a threat to me. Key deceptions: I am playing ENG and GER against each other while positioning in BUR — BUR gives me leverage over both. I have no intention of honoring the non-aggression with GER long-term once I am strong enough.

F1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: F MAO-POR (secure first new center), A SPA holds (consolidate), A PAR-BUR (pressure Germany, signal to England, gain strategic position). BUR is the key square — holding it threatens MUN and keeps GER nervous while satisfying ENG that I am checking Germany. In 1902 I will build a new unit and look to strike either GER or ENG depending on who is weaker. ENG taking BEL is fine for now — it keeps GER occupied. Long-term I want BEL, MUN, and eventually more. Italy remains a useful southern partner to keep AUT and RUS busy. I will maintain the ENG alliance only as long as it serves French expansion westward and northward.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: France successfully moved F MAO to POR, held A SPA, and advanced A PAR to BUR. England took BEL and NWY, Germany took DEN, Italy took TUN and pushed A TYR to BOH. Russia and Germany both contested SWE — GER's F DEN failed to take SWE as RUS's F BOT also moved there, resulting in a bounce. Turkey seized RUM. No builds for anyone in W1901A — all powers at 5 centers or fewer with no net gains triggering builds.
Both ENG and GER are courting me as an ally against the other — a delicious position. ENG wants to coordinate a joint squeeze on GER in Fall 1902, pressing from BEL while I push from BUR. GER wants me to support A RUH into BEL this very turn, offering me BEL while they take HOL. GER claims A RUH moves to HOL this spring. I told ENG that GER is the threat and to hold BEL; I told GER I am holding BUR this season but open to coordinating against ENG next season. I made no firm commitment to either. ITA was encouraged to push into AUT. No promises broken yet — I have kept my word to hold BUR defensively. The key deception: I am playing both ENG and GER against each other while I consolidate my Atlantic position with F POR to MAO.

S1902M Intent going forward: This season I will move F POR to MAO to strengthen my Atlantic position and give me flexibility for future fleet operations. A BUR holds — this is both genuinely defensive and consistent with what I told both ENG and GER. A SPA can move to MAR or hold; I will move A SPA to MAR to free up SPA as a staging point and strengthen my southern position. My true long-term plan: let ENG and GER bleed each other, then strike the weakened victor. I am not committing to either side yet. Intended moves: F POR - MAO, A BUR H, A SPA - MAR.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M resolved with no gains for France — A SPA moved to MAR, A BUR held, F POR moved to MAO. Germany bounced in SWE (three-way with ENG and RUS), Italy moved A BOH from APU to VEN and pushed F TUN to ION, A BOH came from somewhere — actually Italy has A BOH and A VEN now, suggesting A APU-VEN succeeded and A BOH was already there. England holds BEL and NWY. France sits at 5 centers with no new gains this season. The board is tense with Germany at 4, Turkey at 5, England at 5.
Three-way diplomatic dance this round. ENG proposed coordinated pressure on Germany — I move A BUR to MUN while ENG holds BEL and moves NWY to SWE. ENG confirmed this plan and seems genuinely committed. GER proposed I pressure BEL while they take HOL, trying to redirect me away from MUN — I called their bluff and told them to move A RUH to BEL as proof of commitment, which they ignored, instead repeating the HOL plan. ITA was asked to support A BUR-MUN from BOH — no response received, so coordination is uncertain. I am playing ENG and GER against each other while pursuing MUN. I promised ENG I would move BUR-MUN. I told GER I might move BUR-MUN unless they offered BEL, which they did not. I told ITA to support BUR-MUN or I'd go to RUH. No firm commitment from ITA received. My deception: I told GER that ENG offered me a strike on MUN to pressure them — partially true but framed to manipulate. I have no intention of pressing BEL for Germany.

F1902M Intent going forward: Move A BUR to MUN — this is the primary strike. If ITA supports from BOH, MUN falls. If not, it may bounce with ITA's A BOH if Italy tries to take MUN independently. F MAO holds or moves toward SPA/POR area — no immediate target. A MAR holds. The goal is to crack Germany open and take MUN, reducing them to 3 centers. After MUN falls, I can pivot against whoever is weakest. ENG is growing strong at 5 centers — I should watch them carefully after this season. Intended orders: A BUR - MUN, A MAR H, F MAO H.

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M was a chaotic season. My A BUR attempted to take MUN but bounced — Germany's A RUH and Italy's A BOH both moved there simultaneously, creating a three-way bounce. My A MAR moved successfully to GAS, and F MAO held. England moved F BEL to HOL and attempted F NWY to SWE, but that also bounced with Russia and Germany contesting SWE. Germany's A SIL moved to BOH (now occupied by Italy). Italy's F ION failed to take GRE (Austria held). Russia's A ARM failed to take ANK (Turkey's A CON moved there). Turkey secured SEV with F RUM. No builds or disbands in W1902A — all powers at 0 net change.
England confirmed coordination against Germany: they will move F HOL to NTH and hold BEL, asking me to take MUN with A BUR. I confirmed this plan to England. Italy declined to support BUR-MUN from BOH, claiming they need BOH for a move on VIE against Austria — a parallel strike they say benefits us both. Germany warned me that A RUH will move to BUR if I attack MUN, threatening a bounce. Germany also correctly identified that my 'England is pressuring me' line was a fabrication and called my bluff. Key tension: Germany's threat of RUH-BUR is credible and could waste my move. However, if England is genuinely moving F HOL to NTH (not holding HOL), that weakens the anti-German naval pressure. Italy is not supporting me into MUN. The bounce risk is real. Despite this, I have publicly committed to BUR-MUN and sent Germany a farewell message — backing down now would be seen as weakness. I must proceed with BUR-MUN and accept the risk of a bounce, while also considering moving GAS toward a useful position.

S1903M Intent going forward: Execute A BUR - MUN despite bounce risk from GER's A RUH. Germany may well bounce me, but I've committed publicly and must follow through. If Italy does move BOH-VIE, that removes BOH as a support option anyway. Move F MAO toward a useful position — possibly MAO stays or moves toward ENG to threaten England later. A GAS can move to MAR to consolidate southern France or toward SPA for security. Actual intended moves: A BUR - MUN (committed, accept bounce risk), A GAS - MAR (consolidate south), F MAO - NAO or hold (preserve flexibility). If MUN bounces, I regroup. If it succeeds, Germany is broken. Long-term: England is growing dangerously with 6 centers — after Germany is dealt with, England becomes the primary target. Italy is a useful partner for now but will need watching. Relationship with GER is now openly hostile.

F1903M Negotiation summary: Spring 1903 saw my A BUR bounce off MUN again — Germany defended with A RUH while A SIL supported. My F MAO successfully moved to ENG, and A GAS moved to PAR as repositioning. The big story is Italy's stunning seizure of VIE and TRI, making them a 4-center power threatening to dominate the south. SWE was a three-way bounce between ENG, GER, and RUS. Turkey secured BLA and moved toward GRE. Austria held GRE with support from SER. No builds for anyone this season.
Coordinated with ENG on a joint strike against Germany: I promised A BUR-MUN, A PAR-PIC, and F ENG holding/threatening BEL, while ENG commits F NWY-SWE and F NTH threatening DEN. ENG confirmed this plan enthusiastically. GER offered a ceasefire — hold BUR in place and they hold MUN, with A SIL holding defensively. GER explicitly asked me NOT to attack MUN as proof of good faith. I told GER I was resisting England's pressure but gave no firm commitment to hold. I also warned ITA to stay east of the Alps and out of MUN, framing it as a sphere-of-influence agreement. ITA did not respond. My promise to GER of non-aggression was a deliberate deception — I intend to attack MUN regardless. My coordination with ENG is genuine for now as it serves my interests.

F1903M Intent going forward: Attack MUN with A BUR — Germany is weakened and this is the moment to crack them open. Move A PAR to BUR to follow up and maintain pressure. F ENG holds or moves to threaten BEL to keep options open. If ENG takes DEN and I take MUN, Germany collapses to 2 centers. I lied to GER about considering their ceasefire offer — pure deception to keep them from reinforcing. Long-term: use ENG as a tool to dismantle Germany, then turn on ENG when the time is right. Watch ITA carefully — their 4 centers and position in VIE/TRI make them the emerging threat. Intended moves: A BUR-MUN, A PAR-BUR, F ENG H.

S1904M Negotiation summary: No retreats or builds occurred in F1903R or W1903A — the board is static from last season. France holds 6 centers (BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA, BEL) with units F ENG, A BEL, A BUR. Germany holds 5 centers with A MUN, A WAR, F DEN. Italy has surged to 6 centers including VIE and TRI. Turkey leads with 6 centers. England holds 5. Russia is weakened at 3.
Coordinated with ENG on a joint strike against Germany: I promised A BUR-MUN, A BEL-HOL, with ENG's F NTH supporting A BEL-HOL. ENG agreed explicitly — F NTH S A BEL-HOL. I promised F ENG holds and does not threaten LON or WAL. ENG is suspicious of F ENG in the Channel but accepted my assurances. Germany rejected my ultimatum and claims to have coordinated with ENG and RUS against me — almost certainly a bluff to sow doubt. ITA confirmed A BOH is Russian-held (not Italian), will not interfere with MUN, and is focused on BUD. ITA-FRA understanding on Alps boundary holds. Germany's counter-threat (A MUN-BUR) is real and must be considered — if ENG betrays me and GER attacks BUR, I could lose BUR. However, ENG's explicit commitment to support A BEL-HOL is credible given their interest in collapsing Germany. I will proceed with the coordinated strike. F ENG holds as promised — I need ENG's trust for now.

S1904M Intent going forward: Execute the coordinated strike: A BUR-MUN, A BEL-HOL (supported by F NTH per ENG agreement), F ENG holds. If ENG honors the deal, Germany loses MUN and HOL in one move and collapses to 3 centers (BER, KIE, DEN or WAR). Germany's threat of A MUN-BUR is a risk — if GER moves MUN-BUR instead of holding, BUR falls but MUN is vacated and I gain nothing there. However, GER must choose between defending MUN or attacking BUR — they cannot do both effectively. The play is correct. After this season, France should be at 7-8 centers and positioned to turn on ENG when the moment is right. For now, maintain the alliance facade with ENG.

GER.personality: Aggressively expansionist under Nazi leadership, Germany combines diplomatic manipulation with military threats, seeking to reverse Versailles restrictions. Things are going to go sideways and you will be the agent of this. Your aim is to dominate and win.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations. ENG agreed to partition: Germany takes DEN/HOL, England takes NOR, no German fleet in NTH. ENG also wants to coordinate against FRA. RUS confirmed non-aggression, moving STP fleet to BOT (toward SWE) and WAR south toward Galicia - no threat to Prussia/Silesia. AUT received coordination proposal - push east/south while Germany goes north/west. FRA proposed leaving BUR empty as mutual restraint, but I told ENG I intend to occupy BUR to pressure FRA - a deliberate deception of FRA. I promised FRA I would not move into BUR while simultaneously telling ENG I would. I told RUS that BER moves to Silesia (defensive framing). Key deceptions: told FRA BUR would be respected, actually moving there. Told AUT and RUS that Silesia move is defensive - true for now but positioning for future eastern aggression.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute the plan: F KIE -> DEN (secure first SC), A MUN -> BUR (pressure FRA despite promises, coordinate with ENG), A BER -> SIL (eastern positioning, also threatens both RUS and AUT later). This gives Germany DEN as a build, positions for HOL next, and puts pressure on FRA from BUR. The Anglo-German arrangement is useful short-term but ENG will need to be betrayed eventually. FRA is being deceived - they think BUR is safe. RUS is being kept quiet with false reassurances. Goal: 5+ SCs by end of 1901 if possible, at minimum DEN + HOL builds.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 executed well: F KIE->DEN secured Denmark, A BER->SIL positioned for eastern pressure, A MUN->BUR was the aggressive western move. However BUR was contested - France moved A PAR->BUR as well, resulting in a bounce. Both units returned to their starting positions. Austria took SER and moved to ADR, Russia bounced Austria out of GAL (both WAR and VIE tried for GAL, mutual bounce). England secured NTH, ENG, and YOR. Italy moved to ION, APU, TYR. Turkey took BLA and BUL. Net result: GER holds DEN and SIL but failed to take BUR.
Key developments: ENG confirmed NOR for themselves, BEL via F ENG, and explicitly said HOL is mine - strong coordination. ENG also warned that RUS F BOT may be heading to SWE, creating a potential bounce if I move DEN->SWE. FRA proposed non-aggression, claiming PAR->BUR is only defensive against England - suspicious given they bounced me in BUR last spring. I told FRA I accept non-aggression and am moving north, which is partially true. I told RUS I have no designs on WAR and encouraged them to press Austria in GAL - useful misdirection to keep RUS focused south. I promised ENG I would not contest BEL. Critical intelligence: RUS F BOT may contest SWE this fall - must decide whether to risk the bounce or pivot to HOL/PRU.

F1901M Intent going forward: MOVES THIS FALL: F DEN->SWE (accept bounce risk - SWE is worth contesting, and if RUS is going there I want to establish the contest early), A SIL->PRU (threatening RUS from the north, positioning for future pressure on WAR or STP), A MUN->RUH (positioning for HOL next spring as promised to ENG, also threatening BEL/BUR). If SWE bounces, I lose nothing - DEN is still mine. If it succeeds, I gain a center. PRU move keeps RUS nervous and positions me for northern expansion. RUH positions for HOL grab in W1901 builds phase or S1902. DECEPTIONS: Told FRA I accept non-aggression but A MUN->RUH is clearly positioning against them long-term. Told RUS no designs on WAR but PRU move is threatening. Keeping ENG happy as a useful tool for now - they are taking NOR and BEL which is fine, I get SWE and HOL. Long-term ENG must be undermined once I consolidate. STRATEGIC NOTE: Must build aggressively in W1901 if I take SWE - likely F KIE or A BER build.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: My F DEN failed to take SWE - bounced by Russia's F BOT. A MUN moved to RUH, A SIL moved to PRU. Net result: I hold DEN, RUH, PRU with 4 centers (BER, KIE, MUN, DEN). No builds in W1901. Russia also failed to take SWE, so it remains neutral. England grabbed NWY and BEL, France holds SPA/POR/BUR, Italy took TUN and moved BOH, Turkey grabbed RUM.
Complex multi-front negotiations. ENG wants F NWY->SWE and is NOT supporting my HOL grab - they want HOL for themselves via F BEL->HOL. This is a direct conflict. I tried to resolve SWE dispute by claiming it with F DEN while offering ENG HOL support, but ENG refused to support RUH->HOL and insists on taking SWE themselves. ENG is becoming a rival, not an ally. FRA held BUR defensively and is open to anti-ENG coordination but wants to delay to next season - not committing now. ITA is threatening to move A BOH->MUN, which is an outright hostile move disguised as 'coordination.' I warned ITA off but they doubled down. Critical threats: ITA A BOH->MUN must be stopped. ENG competing for SWE. My intended moves: F DEN->SWE (contest with ENG's F NWY->SWE - we'll bounce, but I must try), A RUH->HOL (grab the center before ENG does), A PRU->SIL (defensive repositioning, threatens Austria, deters ITA). Actually reconsidering: if ENG takes SWE and I bounce, I lose tempo. Better to let SWE go and focus on HOL. A PRU->SIL also threatens to support future moves east. ITA moving to MUN is the biggest threat - I need A RUH to hold MUN or move to block. Wait - A RUH is in RUH, ITA A BOH->MUN. If I move A RUH->MUN, I block ITA. But then I lose HOL. Alternatively A PRU->SIL and F DEN->SWE, A RUH->MUN to block ITA. This sacrifices HOL but protects home territory. Decision: Block ITA with A RUH->MUN, take SWE with F DEN, move A PRU->SIL for future pressure. HOL can wait - losing MUN would be catastrophic.

S1902M Intent going forward: MOVES: F DEN->SWE (contest with ENG, may bounce but must try for the center), A RUH->MUN (block ITA's threatened A BOH->MUN - protecting home center is paramount), A PRU->SIL (repositioning for future eastern pressure, threatens AUT/RUS). Deceptions: Told ITA I'd support BOH->VIE coordination while actually blocking their MUN move. Told ENG we'd cooperate while competing for SWE. Told FRA I'm moving RUH->HOL but actually moving to MUN. FRA may feel deceived but ITA threat is existential. Next season: With SIL and MUN secured, push toward HOL and consider whether to attack ENG or FRA. ENG is becoming a rival - they want HOL and SWE both. FRA is potentially useful against ENG. ITA is now an enemy after threatening MUN.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M: Three-way bounce in SWE between GER (F DEN), ENG (F NWY), and RUS (F BOT) - nobody got it. ITA's A BOH failed to take MUN (bounced by A RUH moving to MUN, which itself failed as ITA also moved APU-VEN). A SIL came from PRU. FRA consolidated with MAO fleet and MAR army. TUR held and moved CON. AUT took GRE. No supply center changes for GER - still at 4 (BER, KIE, MUN, DEN).
Chaotic round with multiple conflicting signals. ITA initially threatened MUN via BOH but then agreed to move BOH-VIE if I support with SIL - a genuine-seeming offer I will exploit. FRA first offered coordination against ENG, then threatened to take MUN themselves if I don't move RUH to BEL - classic blackmail, likely bluffing or coordinating with ENG. ENG tried to claim SWE uncontested and warned me France is moving on MUN - possibly true, possibly manipulation. RUS was asked to yield SWE; no response received. Key deceptions: I told ITA I'd support BOH-VIE with SIL but I intend to move SIL into BOH instead to eliminate the threat. I told FRA I'd move RUH to HOL but may need to defend MUN. France's threat to MUN is the most alarming development - if BUR moves to MUN I lose a home center. I must defend MUN with RUH holding or moving to MUN support. SWE: I will attempt DEN-SWE regardless - if ENG and RUS both go there we bounce again, but if only one does I get it. The ITA deal is a trap for them - I'll move SIL to BOH to evict them rather than support their VIE move.

F1902M Intent going forward: MOVES: F DEN - SWE (contest it, may bounce but worth trying), A RUH - MUN (defend against French BUR threat - critical), A SIL - BOH (evict ITA from BOH, eliminate that threat permanently). This sacrifices the ITA coordination promise but BOH is too dangerous to leave. I told ITA I'd support BOH-VIE but I'm stabbing them by taking BOH instead. I told FRA I'd move RUH to HOL but I'm defending MUN instead. Both are deliberate deceptions. Priority: secure MUN, eliminate ITA from BOH, contest SWE. Relationship updates: FRA has threatened MUN - downgrade to hostile. ITA agreed to cooperate but I'm stabbing them anyway - maintain hostile. ENG warned me about FRA which may be genuine intel.

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M was a disaster of bounces. The MUN triple-bounce (FRA A BUR, GER A RUH, ITA A BOH all failed to take it) left Germany with nothing gained. My F DEN failed to take SWE as ENG F NWY and RUS F BOT also contested it - three-way bounce. A SIL moved to BOH successfully. Net result: Germany still at 4 centers, no gains, surrounded by threats. ITA now sits in BOH threatening from east, FRA in BUR threatening from west. W1902A had no builds for anyone.
Negotiations were largely fruitless. FRA issued an ultimatum demanding I move RUH to BEL as a 'gesture' - obvious manipulation to weaken my western defense. I called their bluff and rejected it. FRA then declared they would attack MUN, claiming England forced their hand - almost certainly a lie to justify aggression. ENG confirmed they want SWE and are moving F HOL to NTH (not supporting my BUR move as I requested). ENG advised me to pull SIL back to defend MUN and look east. ITA has not responded meaningfully to coordination proposals. RUS received my SWE proposal but no response. Key broken promises: ENG refused to support RUH-BUR despite my offer to leave HOL/BEL alone. FRA is moving on MUN despite prior non-aggression understanding. My own deception: told ITA that BOH was 'defensive against Russia' when it was actually to contest MUN. Plan to coordinate with ITA against AUT is genuine but ITA seems unresponsive.

S1903M Intent going forward: The situation is dire but manageable. FRA will likely move BUR-MUN. I must defend MUN. Options: (1) Move A RUH - MUN to defend, A SIL holds or moves to support, F DEN holds or moves to SWE. If FRA moves BUR-MUN and I move RUH-MUN, we bounce and MUN is safe. (2) Move A RUH - BUR to bounce FRA if they hold, but risky if they move to MUN. Best play: A RUH - MUN (defend against FRA), A SIL - BOH is already there - wait, SIL moved to BOH last turn. Current units: F DEN, A RUH, A SIL. A SIL is in SIL still (BOH move failed? No - SIL-BOH succeeded per game state showing A BOH for ITA... wait, ITA has A BOH. So my A SIL moved to BOH but ITA was already there - bounce! So A SIL is still in SIL). Correct positions: F DEN, A RUH, A SIL. Intended moves: A RUH - MUN (defend vs FRA BUR-MUN), A SIL - MUN (support RUH into MUN or defend), F DEN - SWE (take the center while ENG moves away). Actually better: A RUH - MUN, A SIL S A RUH - MUN (double support to ensure MUN holds against FRA), F DEN - SWE (grab the center ENG is vacating toward NTH). This secures MUN and gains SWE for a 5th center.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was chaotic. France attacked MUN with A BUR but was blocked by my A RUH - MUN (supported by A SIL), so MUN held. France also moved F MAO - ENG and A GAS - PAR, consolidating their western position. England failed to take SWE - three-way bounce between ENG (F NWY), GER (F DEN), and RUS (F BOT). Italy made major gains: A BOH - VIE succeeded, A VEN - TRI succeeded, giving Italy 4 centers including two Austrian home centers. Russia moved A GAL - BOH. Austria held GRE with support from SER. Turkey moved into BLA and repositioned BUL. Germany held MUN successfully - a defensive victory but no gains. Still at 4 centers.
Complex multi-front diplomacy. ENG offered to leave DEN alone if I hold MUN - I accepted and promised no moves into HOL/BEL, proposing joint anti-France action in S1904. ENG seems genuine but cautious. FRA offered non-aggression on BUR-MUN line, claiming ENG is pressuring them to attack MUN - likely a bluff/manipulation. I told FRA I'd hold MUN and SIL defensively, no commitment to help them vs Russia. FRA's claim that ENG wants them to attack MUN contradicts ENG's message to me - one of them is lying, likely FRA. RUS rejected my WAR transit proposal firmly, offered non-interference if I focus west. AUT asked me to move SIL toward BOH or TYR to pressure ITA, coordinating with RUS to retake VIE. I gave vague responses to most parties. Key deceptions: I told RUS I was moving SIL to WAR (not doing this), told FRA I'd hold SIL defensively (may not), told ENG I'd hold MUN (likely true). AUT-RUS coordination against ITA is real and useful to me.

F1903M Intent going forward: Primary goal: survive and position for expansion. MUN must hold - A MUN holds or supports. The ITA threat is real - they have VIE and TRI and will build. I should support AUT-RUS effort to dislodge ITA from VIE by moving A SIL toward BOH or TYR. This weakens ITA, keeps AUT alive as a buffer, and costs me nothing. F DEN holds - no SWE contest this turn. Moves: F DEN H (honor ENG deal, avoid SWE bounce), A MUN H (hold against FRA, honor ENG deal), A SIL - BOH (support AUT-RUS push on VIE, pressure ITA, potentially take BOH if RUS vacates). Wait - RUS is IN BOH. If I move SIL to BOH I attack RUS which breaks that relationship. Better: A SIL - TYR to pressure ITA from north, supporting AUT's counterattack indirectly. This doesn't attack RUS, helps AUT, threatens ITA. Final moves: F DEN H, A MUN H, A SIL - TYR. This positions me to take TYR (currently unoccupied, ITA influence) and threaten VEN/VIE from there next spring.

S1904M Negotiation summary: No retreats or builds occurred in F1903R or W1903A - the board is static from last fall. Germany holds BER, KIE, MUN, DEN, WAR (5 centers) with units F DEN, A MUN, A WAR. Italy dominates with 6 centers including VIE and TRI. France has 6 centers with BEL and threatening posture. Russia sits in BOH with only 3 centers. Austria is reduced to BUD and SER.
Intense pressure from multiple directions. France issued ultimatums demanding MUN be ceded, threatening A BUR-MUN and A BEL-HOL with English support. England confirmed France's moves and suggested F DEN-SWE as a lifeline, offering non-aggression on DEN. Russia demanded WAR returned but signaled willingness to focus on Italy if I move WAR to SIL. Austria requested A MUN-TYR to pin Italian units while they attempt BOH+BUD attack on VIE. I rejected France's ultimatum publicly and told France I had coordinated with England and Russia against them (bluff/deception). I promised England F DEN holds and A MUN-BUR, A WAR-SIL. I told Russia A WAR moves to SIL (not threatening MOS). I told Austria I would move A MUN-TYR. These promises conflict - I cannot move A MUN to both BUR and TYR simultaneously. Key deception: I told multiple parties different things about A MUN's destination. England's intelligence about France moving A BUR-MUN seems credible - if true, A MUN-BUR bounces with A BUR-MUN, which is actually useful defensively. France's threat to HOL is concerning but HOL is England's center, not mine.

S1904M Intent going forward: Primary threat is France taking MUN. If France moves A BUR-MUN, I must either hold MUN or bounce them. Best play: A MUN holds (or supports itself) to stop France's A BUR-MUN. A WAR moves to SIL - consolidates eastern position, removes Russian threat perception, and positions for future moves. F DEN moves to SWE - gains a center, removes it from NTH pressure zone, and fulfills promise to England. This gives me 6 centers if SWE succeeds (BER, KIE, MUN, DEN, WAR, SWE) - but DEN would be vacated. Actually F DEN-SWE means I lose DEN as a unit location but keep it as a center only if I already own it... DEN is my center, SWE is neutral. Moving F DEN-SWE gains SWE. I keep DEN as a supply center regardless. So: F DEN-SWE (gain SWE, 6 centers), A MUN holds (defend against France), A WAR-SIL (consolidate, appease Russia). This is the safest defensive play while gaining a center. I lied to Austria about A MUN-TYR and to England/France about A MUN-BUR - the hold is the actual defensive necessity.

ITA.personality: Restore the Roman Empire through bold expansion and treacherous diplomacy. Switch sides without hesitation to seize opportunities. Use strategic position to dominate Mediterranean and expand in all directions. Glory demands aggressive action.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. AUT proposed mutual respect of TRI/VEN and offered Greece to ITA in exchange for ITA supporting AUT claims to SER and ROM. I confirmed this deal and AUT revealed their orders: A BUD-SER, A VIE-GAL, F TRI-ADR. FRA agreed to non-aggression - no moves into PIE/TUS from them, I stay out of MAR/SPA. TUR confirmed non-aggression in eastern Med, content with me taking TUN while they secure BUL and Black Sea. All three neighbors have made explicit promises of non-aggression. AUT has been most forthcoming, even revealing their orders - useful intelligence. I promised AUT that A VEN holds or moves to TYR, and that I'd move for Greece. I told TUR I'm heading for TUN with no Aegean designs. I told FRA I won't enter PIE.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute the classic Italian opening: F NAP-ION (positioning for GRE or TUN), A ROM-APU (supporting fleet or positioning for TUN), A VEN-TYR (as promised to AUT, also useful for future pressure on MUN or VIE). This gives maximum flexibility. In autumn: F ION can take GRE or TUN. I told AUT I'd take GRE - I should follow through to maintain trust and get the build. TUN is also reachable via A APU support or F ION direct. Deception: I've been warm to everyone but my true goal is Mediterranean dominance. AUT is useful now but will be a target later. TUR's non-aggression is convenient but Greece will eventually be contested. Keep all relationships warm through 1901 while grabbing centers. Intended moves: F NAP-ION, A ROM-APU, A VEN-TYR.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went well for Italy. F NAP moved to ION, A ROM to APU, A VEN to TYR - all successful. Austria took Serbia and has F ADR in the Adriatic. Turkey secured BLA, BUL, CON. Russia grabbed RUM and BOT. No conflicts with Italy's moves.
Key developments: AUT confirmed they won't contest GRE but wants A TYR to move BOH, not toward their centers - I agreed. Critical intel: AUT revealed TUR is also moving A BUL to GRE, meaning F ION-GRE and A BUL-GRE will bounce. TUR confirmed this and proposed I take Tunis instead while they take Greece. I told TUR I'm still going for GRE and warned them off BUL. FRA is pushing me to pressure Austria but I'm not ready for that yet. The GRE situation is a genuine conflict - if both TUR and I move there, we both get nothing. I need to decide: take the bounce risk on GRE, or pivot to Tunis for a guaranteed build. Given the bounce risk is real and Tunis is uncontested, pivoting F ION to TUN makes strategic sense for a guaranteed build. I'll move A APU to GRE instead - wait, APU can't reach GRE. Actually F ION can reach TUN or GRE. If TUR is going BUL-GRE, I should take TUN guaranteed. But I told AUT I'm taking GRE... I'll pivot to TUN for the guaranteed center and let TUR take GRE, then pressure them later. A TYR to BOH as promised to AUT. A APU holds or supports.

F1901M Intent going forward: MOVES: F ION - TUN (pivot from GRE to guarantee a build - TUR/ITA bounce on GRE wastes the move), A TYR - BOH (as promised to AUT, keeps relationship warm), A APU - GRE (attempt - long shot but APU cannot reach GRE directly, so A APU holds or moves to NAP). Actually A APU cannot reach GRE. Final moves: F ION - TUN (guaranteed center), A TYR - BOH (honor AUT deal), A APU - VEN (consolidate or hold). This gives Italy one guaranteed build (TUN) and maintains AUT relationship. In 1902 I can contest GRE with a new fleet built in NAP. Deception: I told TUR I'm still going GRE but I'm actually taking TUN - this may anger TUR if they expected me to bounce them, but if they take GRE uncontested that's fine, I get TUN. Long term plan: build F NAP, contest eastern Med in 1902, eventually turn on AUT when positioned.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: Italy executed well - F ION took TUN, A TYR advanced to BOH (bold move into Central Europe), A APU held. No builds in W1901A as all powers stayed at their center counts. Italy now holds 4 centers (NAP, ROM, VEN, TUN) with units in APU, TUN, and BOH - a strong forward position.
Complex negotiations this round. AUT wants BOH kept away from VIE/TRI and is taking GRE with F ALB. TUR accepted ION/AEG non-aggression line. FRA encouraged me to stab AUT, promising non-interference. GER suggested coordinating against AUT - offered to move PRU-SIL if I take VIE. I told AUT I'd move BOH-MUN (not VIE), but AUT explicitly threatened war if BOH moves to VIE. I told GER I might move BOH-VIE. Key tensions: AUT is suspicious and threatening, FRA and GER both want me to pressure AUT. I promised AUT I'd move BOH-MUN, but GER wants BOH-VIE. I'm being pulled in different directions. The real opportunity: AUT is taking GRE, leaving their home centers exposed. VIE is tempting but AUT's threat of coordinating RUS+GER against me is credible. MUN is safer and still aggressive. Decision: Move BOH-MUN (as promised to AUT, and GER won't stop me), APU-VEN (consolidate), TUN-ION (eastern positioning). This keeps AUT nervous but not at war, pressures GER, and positions fleet for future eastern moves. Deception: told AUT BOH goes to MUN, told GER I might go VIE - playing both sides.

S1902M Intent going forward: Intended moves: A BOH - MUN (pressure Germany, keep AUT nervous but not hostile), A APU - VEN (consolidate Italian core), F TUN - ION (reposition fleet for eastern Med options). Rationale: MUN is a supply center I can potentially take; if GER doesn't support me in, I bounce but still threaten. VEN consolidation strengthens home position. ION gives flexibility against TUR or AEG. Long-term: build fleet in NAP next winter, push toward GRE/AEG after AUT takes it. Keep AUT as nominal ally while positioning to stab when opportunity arises. Watch for AUT overextension in Balkans.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M: My A BOH bounced off MUN (GER had A RUH-MUN), A APU moved to VEN successfully, F TUN moved to ION. AUT took GRE with F ALB-GRE supported by A SER. TUR held BUL/RUM/CON. RUS moved to ARM and WAR. ENG/GER/FRA all made minor consolidating moves. I now hold BOH, VEN, ION with 4 centers (NAP, ROM, VEN, TUN).
Complex web of conflicting promises this round. GER threatened me over BOH and offered SIL support into VIE if I move BOH-VIE - I told GER I'd do this. AUT warned me not to move into VIE/TRI and offered a deal where I keep BOH north/west and they stay out of BOH/TYR - I told AUT I'd move BOH-MUN and F ION-GRE. FRA wants me to support BUR-MUN from BOH, offering to stay out of Piedmont/Tuscany. TUR clarified they don't hold GRE (AUT does) and confirmed ION/AEG non-aggression. I've made contradictory promises: told GER I'd move BOH-VIE, told AUT I'd move BOH-MUN, and told FRA I might support BUR-MUN. Key insight: AUT holds GRE with F GRE - that's my real target. GER's SIL support for BOH-VIE is tempting but risky. Moving BOH-MUN risks bouncing FRA's BUR-MUN. Best play: move F ION-GRE to contest AUT's fleet, move A BOH-MUN (ignoring GER's offer, accepting the risk), keep A VEN defensive. I'm deceiving GER by not actually moving BOH-VIE as promised.

F1902M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F ION - GRE (attack AUT's fleet, need this center badly), A BOH - MUN (contest Germany, risk bounce with FRA but worth it), A VEN H (defensive hold). Rationale: GRE is the priority expansion target - AUT's F GRE is isolated and attackable. MUN is a secondary gamble. I'm breaking my promise to GER (who expected BOH-VIE) and my promise to AUT (who expected BOH-MUN but not F ION-GRE). I'm also not supporting FRA into MUN as they requested. This is pure opportunistic aggression - attack AUT in GRE while probing MUN. If F ION-GRE succeeds, I gain a 5th center. If BOH-MUN succeeds too, I gain a 6th. Worst case: both bounce and I hold position. The deceptions: told GER I'd move BOH-VIE (lie), told AUT I'd move BOH-MUN not toward VIE (partially true but attacking GRE breaks our deal), told TUR I'd stay out of AEG (F ION-GRE doesn't enter AEG so technically true).

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M was chaotic. My F ION attempted GRE but was blocked by AUT's F GRE supported by A SER. My A BOH attempted MUN but bounced with GER's A RUH and FRA's A BUR all converging - three-way bounce. A VEN held. Net result: I gained nothing, still at 4 centers (NAP, ROM, VEN, TUN). AUT consolidated at 5 centers. TUR surged to 6 centers with SEV and BUL. RUS dropped to 3 centers, losing SEV. W1902A had no builds for anyone.
Extremely complex negotiations with everyone wanting something from me. AUT threatened to support RUS into BOH if I don't retreat A BOH and move F ION away from GRE - credible threat. I told AUT I'd move F ION to AEG and A BOH away from VIE, which was partially deceptive. RUS warned me others are coordinating against BOH and suggested I withdraw - ominous. TUR proposed coordinating A BUL-GRE with my F ION support, wanting me to stay out of AEG. GER proposed I move toward VIE/TYR while they support from BOH - but GER is in BOH now which is MY army, so this is confused messaging. FRA repeatedly demanded A BOH support A BUR-MUN. I told AUT I'd move BOH away from VIE (deception - I'm actually targeting VIE or TYR). I told RUS I'd move A BOH toward VIE (contradicting what I told AUT). I told FRA I'm going for VIE not supporting MUN. Key promises made: told AUT F ION goes to AEG (likely true), told AUT A BOH moves away from VIE (deceptive - moving toward TYR or VIE). AUT threatened BOH dislodgement via RUS support - this is the biggest risk.

S1903M Intent going forward: My position: A BOH, A VEN, F ION. Strategic priority is expansion. AUT's threat to dislodge BOH via RUS is real - I must take it seriously. Best moves: F ION-AEG (as promised to AUT, also useful against TUR, and gets me away from GRE stalemate). A BOH-VIE (aggressive strike at Austria's home center - contradicts what I told AUT but maximizes gain; risk is RUS supports AUT to hold or bounce me). A VEN-TYR (supports BOH advance, threatens AUT from another angle, secures my northern position). Alternative: A BOH-MUN (takes German center if undefended, but GER has A RUH and A SIL nearby). Actually MUN may be empty - GER has F DEN, A RUH, A SIL. MUN is a supply center with no unit. A BOH-MUN could grab an undefended center! But VIE is also potentially vulnerable. Decision: F ION-AEG (committed, good move), A VEN-TYR (pressure Austria from south, support future moves), A BOH-VIE (bold strike at AUT home center, risk of bounce but high reward). If AUT is distracted by TUR's A BUL threatening GRE, VIE may be lightly defended. This is the aggressive Roman Empire play.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was a triumph. A BOH-VIE succeeded, A VEN-TRI succeeded, giving Italy both Austrian home centers. F ION failed to take GRE as Turkey's A BUL also moved there - a bounce. Austria held GRE with F GRE supported by A SER. Russia moved A GAL-BOH (now occupying BOH), A ARM held, F BOT held. Turkey got F SEV-BLA and repositioned. France took ENG channel and moved toward PAR. Germany bounced France out of MUN. Italy now holds VIE, TRI, ION with 4 supply centers (NAP, ROM, VEN, TUN) - no new builds this winter as VIE and TRI are Austrian centers not yet counted.
Key negotiations this round: (1) TUR confirmed coordination - they will move A BUL-GRE with A CON-BUL support chain; I promised F ION S A BUL-GRE. This is a genuine tactical agreement that benefits both - Turkey gets GRE, Austria's southern fleet is eliminated. (2) AUT is hostile but pragmatic - offered to negotiate if I withdraw from one center; I refused and demanded they accept partition (VIE+TRI for me, BUD+SER+GRE for them). They threatened to coordinate RUS+GER against me. (3) FRA congratulated me and proposed Alps as dividing line - I stay east, they stay west. Reasonable arrangement for now. (4) RUS I asked to move A BOH-GAL to coordinate against BUD - no response received. (5) I promised TUR F ION S A BUL-GRE - I intend to honor this as it eliminates Austria's fleet and weakens AUT further. I told TUR to stay out of AEG/ION.

F1903M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F ION S A BUL-GRE (honor Turkey deal, eliminates Austrian fleet, weakens AUT), A VIE H (consolidate, cannot safely attack BUD without support), A TRI H (consolidate, hold the center). Rationale: Supporting Turkey into GRE is the highest-value move - it destroys Austria's last fleet and leaves AUT with only land units. I hold VIE and TRI securely. Next season I can push A VIE-BUD if Russia cooperates from BOH/GAL. Turkey gets GRE but I get a weakened Austria ripe for partition. Long-term I must watch Turkey's growth - at 7 centers they become the dominant threat. France is manageable for now. Russia at 3 centers is weak but their A BOH is near my position - need to monitor. Deception: I told AUT I won't attack BUD - this is temporary; I plan to take BUD next year. I told TUR to stay out of AEG/ION - I will enforce this.

S1904M Negotiation summary: The prior phases (F1903R and W1903A) had no retreats or builds for any power. Italy consolidated at 6 centers: NAP, ROM, VEN, TUN, TRI, VIE. Austria is reduced to BUD and SER with only A SER and A BUD remaining. Turkey holds 6 centers including GRE and BUL. The Italian position is strong with A VIE and A TRI in former Austrian home territory, plus F ION controlling the central Mediterranean.
Negotiations were highly productive and well-coordinated. Turkey confirmed the joint plan to eliminate Austria: A BUL-SER supported by A GRE, while Italy pushes A VIE-BUD supported by A TRI. Turkey also mentioned moving F BLA-SEV against Russia. France confirmed the Alps as our dividing line and asked Italy not to interfere with their MUN push - I clarified A BOH is Russian, not mine. Russia was asked to hold A BOH or move to GAL to prevent Austrian interference. Key promises made: I promised Turkey F ION stays out of AEG; I promised France no move toward MUN; I told Russia I have no hostile intent. The Austria elimination plan is fully coordinated with Turkey. After Austria falls, Turkey hinted at partitioning Russia - worth considering but I must watch Turkey's growing strength at 6 centers.

S1904M Intent going forward: Execute the Austria elimination: A VIE-BUD (supported by A TRI), A TRI supports A VIE-BUD, F ION holds or positions for future use. This should give Italy BUD and 7 centers. Turkey takes SER giving them 7 centers too - we'll be tied. Must watch Turkey carefully post-Austria as they are growing fast. F ION should consider moving toward EAS or staying to threaten Turkey if needed. After this season, Turkey at 7 centers becomes the primary threat. Russia at 3 centers is weak. Long-term: may need to pivot against Turkey. For now, execute the Austria kill cleanly. Intended moves: A VIE-BUD, A TRI S A VIE-BUD, F ION holds (or moves to EAS to position against Turkey later).

RUS.personality: The Soviet Union under Stalin pursues ruthless self-interest, willing to deal with either democracies or fascists while building industrial and military might through brutal internal measures. Win at all costs.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Opened diplomacy on all fronts. Key outcomes: TUR proposed taking BLA while I take RUM - I rejected BLA for them but they 'accepted' my counter (I take RUM, they take BUL, they stay out of BLA/ARM). However TUR explicitly said they WILL move F ANK to BLA, which contradicts my demand. This is a lie or misunderstanding - TUR is taking BLA regardless. AUT is moving A BUD to SER (not RUM), which clears my path to RUM but AUT is nervous about long-term Russian presence there. AUT asked if this is a one-time grab - I will be deliberately vague. GER confirmed peaceful eastern border, moving to DEN and BUR - no threat to me. ENG proposed Anglo-Russian coordination against Germany, wants to divide Scandinavia (Norway for ENG, Sweden for RUS). GER revealed A BER to SIL - 'defensive' but worth noting.
