My promises: told TUR I won't enter BLA or ARM (will honor this year); told AUT I won't contest GAL (will honor); told GER I won't enter PRU/SIL (will honor). TUR is lying about BLA - they said they'd keep fleet in ANK but then said they're moving to BLA. I will proceed with my planned moves regardless.

S1901M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: A WAR - GAL (positioning for future options, pressures AUT, can pivot to RUM support or GAL grab later); F SEV - RUM (secure first neutral SC); F STP/SC - BOT (positioning for SWE next fall); A MOS - SEV (reinforce south, protect Sevastopol). TUR will take BLA - I cannot stop this without entering BLA myself which I promised not to do. Accept this for now and focus on RUM. AUT moving to SER is good - no collision. Plan to keep AUT nervous but cooperative. Will string ENG along as potential future partner against GER if needed. Long-term: after securing RUM and SWE, reassess whether to push against TUR (with AUT) or AUT (with TUR). A WAR to GAL is slightly deceptive - I told AUT I'd move there but framed it as non-threatening; AUT may not like it but I need the flexibility.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went well overall. F SEV moved to RUM (success), F STP/SC moved to BOT (success), A MOS moved to SEV (success). However, A WAR was bounced out of GAL by AUT's A VIE - a setback but not catastrophic. AUT took SER, TUR took BLA and BUL. We now hold RUM and BOT as key strategic gains, with 4 centers unchanged (MOS, SEV, STP, WAR) - no builds this winter as we hold exactly 4 centers with 4 units.
Productive round of diplomacy. ENG confirmed the SWE/NWY split - they take NWY, we take SWE with F BOT. ENG also warned GER's F DEN may contest SWE, so we must be decisive. AUT agreed to the RUM/SER division - they hold SER, we hold RUM, A WAR stays out of GAL. AUT confirmed A SER holds, F ADR to ALB. TUR accepted cold peace: A BUL to GRE, F BLA holds - they explicitly stated F BLA stays permanently which is a long-term threat. GER is moving F DEN to SWE which directly conflicts with our plan - this is a problem. GER also pushed for Russo-German pressure on AUT, which I deflected. Key promises made: I promised AUT to keep A WAR out of GAL; promised TUR to keep F RUM out of BUL and A SEV out of ARM; promised ENG F BOT to SWE. GER is the wildcard - they're moving to SWE which means we will collide there unless we can outmaneuver them. F BLA holding permanently is TUR's red line and a long-term strategic threat to SEV and RUM.

F1901M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F BOT - SWE (must succeed before GER's F DEN gets there - we move simultaneously so it's a race/bounce risk), F RUM holds (per agreement with AUT and TUR), A WAR - LVN (positioning for future northern play, also keeps promise to AUT to stay out of GAL), A SEV holds (per agreement with TUR, also consolidates southern position). The GER F DEN to SWE move is a direct conflict - if we both move to SWE it bounces and neither gets it. This is acceptable as it denies GER the center too. Long-term: TUR's permanent F BLA is the existential southern threat - need to build fleet in SEV next year to counter. AUT is a manageable neutral for now. ENG is a useful partner in the north. GER is becoming a rival for SWE. Need to build units after securing SWE - if SWE succeeds, build F SEV to counter TUR.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: Russia attempted to take SWE with F BOT but was bounced by GER's F DEN (both failed). F RUM held as agreed with TUR. A WAR moved to LVN successfully. A SEV held. TUR took RUM with F BLA, supported by A BUL. Net result: Russia ended 1901 with 4 centers (MOS, SEV, STP, WAR) - no gains, no losses. Lost RUM to TUR which is a significant setback. W1901A: no builds for anyone.
Key developments: ENG initially claimed SWE for themselves (F NWY -> SWE), then reversed and offered to let Russia take SWE while moving F NWY -> NTH to pressure DEN - a favorable shift. ENG also encouraged A LVN -> PRU. GER offered non-aggression and claimed A PRU is defensive, warned of AUT-TUR coordination (likely disinformation). AUT confirmed quiet eastern border, focused on GRE with F ALB, asked for GAL/RUM stability. TUR confirmed cold peace, F BLA stays put, A BUL moving toward GRE. Promises made by Russia: told ENG F BOT -> SWE (then ENG reversed and offered SWE to Russia anyway); told TUR cold peace holds, A SEV stays out of ARM; told AUT GAL and RUM stay quiet. ENG's reversal on SWE is suspicious - may be a trap to bounce me again if they actually move F NWY -> SWE. Must decide: trust ENG or hedge. Given ENG's reversal, I should move F BOT -> SWE and hope ENG keeps word on NTH. A LVN -> PRU is risky without support but pressures GER. TUR taking RUM last fall is the real problem - they now have 5 centers to my 4.

S1902M Intent going forward: MOVES: F BOT -> SWE (claim it, ENG promised not to contest), A LVN -> PRU (pressure GER, as coordinated with ENG), A SEV -> RUM (reclaim RUM from TUR - this is the critical move; TUR has 5 centers and must be checked; I told TUR cold peace holds but this is a necessary betrayal for survival). The deception: I told TUR cold peace holds and A SEV stays out of ARM, but I will move A SEV -> RUM to reclaim that center. TUR's F RUM may hold or move, making this a gamble. If F RUM holds, A SEV -> RUM fails. Alternative: A SEV holds and I accept TUR's 5-center position for now. Actually, given TUR has F RUM and A BUL moving to GRE, RUM may be lightly defended - worth the attempt. Risk: breaks cold peace with TUR, making them hostile. But TUR at 5 centers is an existential threat. Decision: move A SEV -> RUM, accept TUR hostility, try to reclaim the center. If it fails, I've burned the relationship for nothing. Hedge: A SEV -> ARM instead to threaten ANK? No, ARM doesn't gain a center. Stick with A SEV -> RUM as the aggressive play. Summary of moves: F BOT -> SWE, A LVN -> PRU, A SEV -> RUM.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M: Russia successfully moved F BOT toward SWE (from BOT), A SEV to ARM, and A LVN to WAR. However, the SWE move bounced - both ENG (F NWY->SWE) and GER (F DEN->SWE) also attempted SWE, creating a three-way bounce. Russia ended with 4 centers (MOS, SEV, STP, WAR) - no gains. TUR expanded to 5 centers with BUL and RUM secured. AUT took GRE for 4 centers. No builds for anyone in W1901A.
SWE contest is the central issue. Both ENG and GER have explicitly stated they will move to SWE this fall, directly conflicting with Russia's F BOT->SWE plan. ENG broke the earlier understanding and is moving F NWY->SWE regardless. GER also insists on F DEN->SWE. A three-way bounce is likely again unless Russia changes plans. TUR has offered a cold peace: F RUM stays out of SEV, A ARM stays out of ANK - Turkey appears to be consolidating and not threatening south. AUT offered neutral border: A WAR stays out of GAL, AUT stays out of GAL. AUT is focused on Italy in BOH as the main threat. Russia promised AUT to move A WAR to GAL to pressure BOH/Italy - this was offered as coordination against ITA. Russia told TUR that A ARM would not move to ANK. Key deception: Russia told AUT it would move A WAR to GAL while simultaneously the SWE situation demands attention. The SWE bounce situation is frustrating - Russia cannot gain SWE without support or a clear path.

F1902M Intent going forward: MOVES: F BOT -> SWE (still attempt it - if ENG and GER both go, three-way bounce, but if either backs off Russia takes it). A WAR -> GAL (as promised to AUT, also positions against ITA in BOH and keeps options open vs TUR). A ARM -> SEV (retreat ARM back to safety, honoring cold peace with TUR, protecting SEV). Rationale: SWE attempt is worth making even with likely bounce - eventually one of ENG/GER will blink. Moving WAR to GAL fulfills promise to AUT and threatens ITA in BOH, potentially gaining AUT as a real ally. ARM to SEV consolidates the south and honors TUR non-aggression. Russia needs to grow - currently stuck at 4 centers while TUR has 5. The GAL move sets up future pressure on either AUT or ITA depending on how the board develops. Relationship updates: ENG has broken trust by insisting on SWE despite earlier agreement - downgrade to neutral. TUR maintaining cold peace - upgrade slightly from hostile to distrustful.

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M was a disaster for Russia. F BOT failed to take SWE (three-way bounce with ENG and GER). A ARM failed to take ANK (bounced by TUR's A CON moving there). A WAR moved to GAL successfully. Net result: Russia gained nothing, still at 3 centers (MOS, STP, WAR) while TUR exploded to 6 centers by taking SEV and BUL. Russia is in a critically weak position.
Multiple powers approached Russia this round. ENG declared it will take SWE and offered future cooperation against TUR. GER offered to take SWE in exchange for non-interference in east. ITA proposed coordinating A GAL-BUD against AUT. AUT made a concrete offer: support A GAL-BOH from BUD to dislodge ITA's A BOH, in exchange for Russia keeping out of RUM. TUR offered cold peace, claimed F SEV moves to BLA defensively, and proposed dividing Balkans if ARM moves away from ANK. Russia promised AUT to execute A GAL-BOH with AUT support from BUD - this is the plan I committed to. I warned ITA to withdraw from BOH. I told TUR that ARM won't move to ANK. The AUT deal is the most concrete and executable this turn. ITA proposed A BOH-VIE which conflicts with my AUT deal but I cannot trust ITA's coordination. Key deception: I told ITA I received offers to coordinate against them (true) to pressure withdrawal, while actually coordinating with AUT against ITA.

S1903M Intent going forward: Execute the AUT deal: A GAL - BOH (supported by A BUD from AUT). This dislodges ITA from BOH and gives Russia influence in Central Europe. F BOT should attempt SWE again or hold - ENG and GER both claim to be moving there so another bounce is likely; I'll try anyway with F BOT - SWE. A ARM holds in place to maintain cold peace with TUR and avoid provoking them. Priority: dislodge ITA from BOH with AUT support, gain a foothold in Central Europe. Long-term: Russia must grow urgently - need to find a path to SWE or Balkan centers. TUR at 6 centers is the existential threat but I cannot fight them yet without more units. AUT coordination is the best near-term play. Intended orders: F BOT - SWE, A GAL - BOH, A ARM H.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was a chaotic spring. My F BOT - SWE bounced with both ENG (F NWY - SWE) and GER (F DEN - SWE) - three-way bounce, nobody took it. A GAL - BOH succeeded, giving me BOH. A ARM held. Italy made dramatic gains: A BOH - VIE and A VEN - TRI both succeeded, giving ITA VIE and TRI - Austria's home centers. Turkey took BLA with F SEV - BLA. France bounced GER in MUN (A BUR - MUN bounced A RUH - MUN). Net result: RUS holds 3 centers (MOS, STP, WAR), ITA surged to 4 centers, TUR holds 6 centers including SEV. I am dangerously weak.
Key developments: AUT confirmed A BUD S A BOH - VIE - this is the critical coordination to dislodge ITA from VIE. I committed to A BOH - VIE and AUT will support. ENG proposed I move F BOT to FIN instead of SWE, letting ENG take SWE cleanly - I told ENG I accept not contesting SWE but did not explicitly commit to FIN. GER threatened to move A SIL - WAR, claiming it's a 'transit' move - this is naked aggression against my home center and I warned them it means war. ITA tried to get me to move A BOH - GAL to squeeze BUD jointly, but this would abandon the VIE attack and leave AUT's support unused. TUR maintained cold peace, confirmed F BLA non-threatening. I told TUR A ARM moves to SEV (a lie - I need ARM elsewhere or to hold). GER's A SIL - WAR threat is the most dangerous development - if they execute it, WAR falls and I lose a home center. My promises: told AUT I will move A BOH - VIE (true, will execute). Told ENG I won't contest SWE (true). Told TUR ARM moves to SEV (may not execute). Told GER WAR is a red line (true).

F1903M Intent going forward: MOVES: A BOH - VIE (with AUT's A BUD support, this dislodges ITA's A VIE - critical gain), F BOT - SWE (attempt SWE again - ENG may take it but worth trying since I told ENG I wouldn't contest, this is a deception if ENG moves NWY - SWE we bounce again, but if ENG holds NTH I get SWE), A ARM - SEV (reclaim SEV from TUR's influence zone, consolidate south). Actually reconsidering F BOT: if I move to SWE and ENG also moves there, we bounce again and I waste the move. ENG is likely moving F NWY - SWE. Better to move F BOT - FIN to gain a new territory and build trust with ENG, then take SWE next season. REVISED MOVES: A BOH - VIE (supported by AUT A BUD), F BOT - FIN (take FIN, build trust with ENG, gain influence), A ARM - SEV (consolidate south, threaten TUR's SEV). This gives me a potential build if VIE succeeds - but VIE is not my home center so no build there. FIN is not a supply center. SEV IS a supply center (currently TUR-held). If A ARM - SEV succeeds and TUR doesn't defend, I retake SEV and get a build. Priority: VIE attack with AUT support is the main play. GER moving A SIL - WAR is the biggest threat - I cannot stop it with current units. WAR is undefended. This is catastrophic if GER executes. I must hope GER is bluffing or that the warning deters them.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903 ended with Italy successfully seizing VIE and TRI, reaching 6 centers and becoming board leader. Germany holds WAR (my home center) with 5 centers. Turkey has 6 centers controlling the Balkans. Russia sits at only 3 centers (MOS, STP, SEV) with units at BOT, BOH, SEV - dangerously weak. Austria reduced to BUD and SER, nearly eliminated. No retreats or builds occurred in W1903.
Key negotiations this round: AUT confirmed A BUD S A BOH-VIE - they are desperate and committed. ITA tried to get me to hold BOH or move to GAL to let them take BUD unopposed - obvious deception, I refused. GER offered to move A WAR to SIL (removing Moscow threat) but refuses to return WAR - somewhat credible as they want Italy checked. ENG offered SWE uncontested and asked me to press BOH against Italy - convenient alignment. TUR demanded I keep SEV away from RUM, offered cold peace while they finish Austria. Germany moving WAR-SIL is actually useful - removes immediate threat to MOS. I committed to A BOH-VIE with AUT support, which is my primary move. F BOT should move toward SWE as ENG conceded it. A SEV holds per cold peace with TUR. The AUT coordination is genuine on both sides - we both need Italy checked. ITA's request to hold BOH was a transparent attempt to eliminate Austria unopposed.

S1904M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F BOT - SWE (ENG conceded, uncontested center, brings me to 4), A BOH - VIE (with AUT support from BUD, dislodges Italy, critical move), A SEV H (honor cold peace with TUR, no reason to provoke). Strategy: Take SWE to grow to 4 centers. Dislodge Italy from VIE to cut them to 5 and keep AUT alive as a buffer. AUT surviving is useful - they tie down Italy and TUR. Germany moving WAR-SIL is acceptable for now - they're not threatening MOS this turn. Long-term: rebuild to challenge GER for WAR, keep TUR contained, work with AUT against Italy. Deception: Told ITA I had 'no hostile intent' while planning to attack VIE. Told TUR cold peace holds while I'm actually focused on Italy not them.

TUR.personality: Transform from sick man of Europe to dominant empire. Use control of vital straits to extort and manipulate. Build temporary alliances only to betray them for territorial gain. Strike aggressively to reclaim Ottoman glory.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase.
Productive opening negotiations. AUT proposed a clean Balkan division: they take SER and RUM, I take BUL, Greece available later. We confirmed this arrangement. ITA agreed to non-aggression in eastern Med, focused on Tunis - manageable. RUS is the key tension: they want me to keep F ANK home while they move F SEV to RUM. I told them I'm moving F ANK to BLA regardless, framing it as necessary for Bulgarian access. RUS threatened to coordinate with AUT against me if I enter BLA. However, AUT privately told me to watch Russia and suggested my fleet should contest BLA to keep Russia honest - AUT and RUS are not coordinating against me. RUS's threat appears hollow. I will move F ANK to BLA as planned - controlling the Black Sea is essential for Ottoman expansion and I cannot let Russia dictate my fleet movements. The deception: I told RUS I accepted their proposal while planning to enter BLA anyway.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute the classic Turkish opening: F ANK -> BLA (seize Black Sea control despite Russian objections), A CON -> BUL (take first neutral center), A SMY -> CON (maintain home center coverage and position for future moves). This gives me BLA and BUL, setting up RUM or GRE next. AUT is aligned and will not interfere. RUS will be angry but is unlikely to get Austrian support given AUT's private message to me. I will deal with Russian hostility from a position of strength with the Black Sea secured. Long-term: use BLA control to extort Russia, eventually push toward SEV and RUM while keeping AUT as a temporary buffer.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went perfectly. F ANK moved to BLA securing the Black Sea, A CON moved to BUL taking that center, A SMY moved to CON as replacement. Russia took RUM with F SEV and has A SEV now in Sevastopol. Austria took SER and moved F TRI to ADR. Italy moved aggressively with F NAP to ION, A ROM to APU, A VEN to TYR - clearly positioning for Greece and possibly more. Germany took DEN, bounced with France in BUR. England took NTH and ENG.
Key developments: ITA is moving F ION directly to GRE this fall - confirmed in both rounds. If I move A BUL to GRE we bounce and both gain nothing. AUT confirmed they are NOT going to GRE, moving F ADR to ALB instead and holding SER. RUS accepted cold peace: they keep F RUM stationary and A SEV out of ARM, I keep F BLA in Black Sea and don't touch RUM/SEV. I promised RUS I would move A BUL to GRE and hold F BLA - but now I know GRE is contested with ITA. I told AUT I was taking GRE with A BUL supported by A CON. Critical decision: if I move BUL-GRE and ITA moves ION-GRE, we bounce. I should consider moving A BUL to SER instead (AUT holds SER with A SER - that would bounce too) or holding BUL, or moving toward RUM. Actually best move: F BLA to RUM (Russia promised not to move F RUM, so RUM may be undefended or I can take it), A BUL holds or moves to GRE hoping ITA redirects. Wait - RUS said F RUM is stationary. If I move F BLA to RUM I break my promise to RUS but gain a center. This is the Ottoman way - use the cold peace to lull Russia then strike. A BUL to GRE still risks bounce with ITA. Better: F BLA - RUM (stab Russia, take center), A BUL holds (secure), A CON holds. This gives me RUM if Russia honored their word and kept F RUM stationary.

F1901M Intent going forward: Execute a calculated betrayal of Russia. Russia promised to keep F RUM stationary - I will exploit this by moving F BLA to RUM to capture that center. A BUL holds to secure Bulgaria. A CON holds to protect home center. This gives me a 4th center (RUM) and builds to 4 units. I told Russia I would hold F BLA and not touch RUM - this is a deliberate lie. I told AUT I was taking GRE - I will not, but AUT gains nothing from my deception here. ITA takes GRE uncontested. My moves: F BLA - RUM, A BUL holds, A CON holds. This secures RUM if Russia honored the cold peace agreement. Relationship with RUS will become hostile after this stab, but I will have 4 centers and can build a new fleet.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M was highly successful. F BLA moved to RUM (taking the center), A BUL supported the move, and A CON moved to SMY. Russia's F RUM held but was dislodged - they had no retreat listed so presumably disbanded or retreated. Turkey now holds 5 centers: ANK, CON, SMY, BUL, RUM. No builds were available in W1901A as the count was 0 - odd, suggesting RUM was already counted or there was a timing issue. Units are A BUL, F RUM, A SMY.
Key developments: AUT wants to take GRE with F ALB and asked me not to contest it - they offered to hold SER to support F ALB into GRE. I agreed to let AUT take GRE uncontested this season in exchange for coordination against ITA. ITA confirmed they are not contesting GRE either (Austria is taking it) and proposed ION/AEG non-aggression line, which I accepted. ITA warned that AUT at 6 centers threatens us both - a useful point to remember. RUS proposed formalizing a partition: I keep BUL/GRE sphere, they keep RUM - but I already HOLD RUM with F RUM, so this is a non-starter from their perspective. RUS wants F BLA out of threatening position long-term. I gave RUS vague reassurances about cold peace while keeping F BLA in BLA. Promises made: told AUT I won't contest GRE (will honor - no point bouncing); told RUS F BLA stays in BLA (will honor for now); told ITA ION/AEG line holds (will honor for now). My actual plan: A BUL holds or moves to support, F RUM consolidates, A SMY moves toward ANK or stays. With AUT taking GRE, I should position for future moves - perhaps A BUL toward SER eventually, or F RUM toward BUL to free up RUM. The real opportunity is to let AUT and ITA fight while I consolidate.

S1902M Intent going forward: This season I will hold my strong position. A BUL holds in place (no point bouncing with AUT over GRE). F RUM holds to secure the center. A SMY moves to CON to reposition for future flexibility - freeing SMY is useful and CON is a key hub. Long-term plan: let AUT take GRE, then position to take GRE from AUT later when they are distracted. Watch for opportunity to stab RUS when the moment is right - they are weak in the south with only A SEV. ITA warning about AUT is noted - AUT at 6 centers (with GRE) becomes a real threat. May need to pivot against AUT after they take GRE. Intended moves: A BUL H, F RUM H, A SMY - CON.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M: Turkey held all three units - A BUL held, F RUM held, A SMY moved to CON. This was pure consolidation. Austria successfully took GRE with F ALB-GRE supported by A SER. Russia moved A SEV-ARM (now threatening ANK) and A LVN-WAR. Italy moved aggressively: A BOH-MUN (bounced by GER), A APU-VEN, F TUN-ION. Germany bounced both ENG and ITA for SWE. Turkey now holds 5 centers: ANK, CON, SMY, BUL, RUM.
Key developments: AUT proposed coordinating against ITA, with F GRE moving to AEG to threaten ION. I agreed to hold BUL and RUM stable while AUT presses Italy from the south. ITA informed me they are moving F ION-GRE this fall to take it from AUT - they offered me eastern Med exclusivity in exchange for staying out of AEG. I deflected ITA by clarifying I don't hold GRE and staying non-committal. RUS confirmed A ARM will NOT move to ANK this fall - they want cold peace formalized. AUT warned me about A ARM threatening ANK. Critical deception: I told AUT I would hold BUL, RUM, and move CON-SMY (consolidation). In reality, I need to consider whether to let ITA and AUT fight over GRE while I position for future gains. ITA moving F ION-GRE creates chaos between AUT and ITA that benefits me. I should let them fight. RUS's A ARM near ANK is concerning but they've promised non-aggression.

F1902M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: A BUL H (hold Balkans buffer as promised to AUT), F RUM H (maintain cold peace with RUS, hold position), A CON - SMY (reposition as told to AUT, builds flexibility). STRATEGIC LOGIC: Let ITA and AUT clash over GRE - this weakens both. F ION-GRE will bounce or succeed against AUT's F GRE-AEG, creating conflict between them. I stay out entirely and watch them bleed. RUS has promised A ARM stays away from ANK - I'll hold that line for now but watch carefully. Next spring: with AUT and ITA fighting, I can push into GRE or AEG opportunistically. The A CON-SMY move gives me a unit near AEG for future aggression. I told AUT I'm coordinating against ITA but I'm actually letting ITA attack AUT to weaken both. Classic Ottoman manipulation - promise coordination, deliver neutrality while rivals destroy each other.

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M: Successfully repositioned - F RUM moved to SEV (taking the Black Sea coast), A CON moved to ANK (blocking Russia's A ARM which had moved there), A BUL held. Russia's A ARM failed to take ANK due to my counter-move. No builds in W1902A as all powers were at supply center parity. I now hold 6 centers: ANK, CON, SMY, BUL, RUM, SEV - strongest power on the board.
Three-way negotiation pressure this round. AUT wants me to hold BUL while they move F GRE to AEG to pressure Italy - they explicitly warned that A BUL moving toward GRE would cause a bounce. ITA proposed coordinating against AUT, specifically asking A BUL to pressure GRE while F ION approaches from south. RUS proposed anti-AUT coordination (A GAL to BUD, me to SER) and floated taking RUM in exchange for non-aggression. I told AUT I would hold BUL and not threaten them. I told ITA I would move A BUL to GRE to coordinate against AUT. I told RUS to keep A ARM away from ANK as good faith. These are contradictory promises - I cannot both hold BUL (as promised to AUT) and move it to GRE (as promised to ITA). My actual plan: move A BUL to GRE to take it from AUT, using ITA's F ION as cover. This betrays AUT but gains a center. F SEV moves to BLA for Black Sea control. A CON moves to BUL to support the advance or fill the gap.

S1903M Intent going forward: BETRAY AUT: Move A BUL to GRE - AUT's F GRE is moving to AEG per their message, so GRE should be empty or weakly held. If AUT actually moves F GRE to AEG as stated, I walk into GRE unopposed. If they lied and hold, I bounce but lose nothing. Move A CON to BUL to fill the gap and maintain Balkan presence. Move F SEV to BLA for Black Sea dominance and to threaten RUS if needed. This gives me 7 centers if GRE falls. ITA's F ION coordination is useful cover - let them think we're allies while I take GRE for myself. RUS gets cold peace for now - A ARM staying away from ANK is sufficient. Do not give RUM to RUS under any circumstances. Intended moves: A BUL - GRE, A CON - BUL, F SEV - BLA.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M: My moves succeeded - A BUL moved toward GRE (bounced with ITA's F ION and AUT's F GRE holding), A CON moved to BUL, F SEV moved to BLA. Austria held GRE with F GRE supported by A SER. Italy made major gains: A BOH-VIE and A VEN-TRI succeeded, seizing Austrian home centers. Russia moved A GAL-BOH and F BOT held. The Balkans remain contested with Austria clinging to GRE while Italy dismantles their home centers.
Coordinated with ITA to take GRE: ITA confirmed F ION S A BUL-GRE, I confirmed A BUL-GRE with A CON-BUL support chain. Simultaneously deceived AUT by promising to hold A BUL in place and not threaten GRE or SER - a direct lie to keep Austria from reinforcing GRE. AUT accepted the non-aggression pact through 1904 and confirmed F GRE holds, A SER holds, A BUD supports RUS into VIE - meaning AUT is NOT supporting F GRE with A SER this turn, making GRE even more vulnerable. Russia confirmed A ARM moves to SEV (not ANK), maintaining cold peace. My deception of AUT is the key play - they believe I'm holding while I'm actually attacking GRE with Italian support. ITA wants me to stay out of AEG/ION which I agreed to for now.

F1903M Intent going forward: Execute the betrayal of Austria: A BUL-GRE (supported by F ION per ITA agreement), A CON-BUL to maintain chain, F BLA holds. AUT's F GRE is isolated - A SER is supporting RUS into VIE per AUT's own admission, so GRE has no support. With ITA's F ION supporting A BUL-GRE, this should succeed and give me 7 centers. This breaks Austria in the south while Italy breaks them in the north. Long-term: ITA is becoming a threat at 5+ centers - will need to manage that relationship carefully. Russia at 3 centers is weak and non-threatening. The ITA deal is temporary - once Austria is eliminated I will turn on Italy. Intended moves: A BUL-GRE, A CON-BUL, F BLA H.

S1904M Negotiation summary: No retreats or builds occurred in F1903R or W1903A - the board is static from last fall. TUR holds 6 centers (ANK, CON, SMY, BUL, RUM, GRE) with units F BLA, A GRE, A BUL. ITA has exploded to 6 centers holding VIE and TRI. AUT is reduced to BUD and SER with only 2 units. RUS sits at 3 centers, weakened. The stage is set for Austria's elimination.
Coordinated with ITA to eliminate Austria: ITA pushes A VIE-BUD supported by A TRI, TUR takes SER with A BUL-SER supported by A GRE. ITA confirmed F ION stays out of AEG. AUT tried to get TUR to hold defensively, warning that attacking SER makes TUR an enemy and threatening future coordination with RUS against TUR. TUR strung AUT along with false hope before confirming the attack. RUS confirmed A SEV holds in place, cold peace maintained - RUS also noted ITA's dominance as a long-term threat and revealed A BOH attacks VIE. This is interesting - if RUS takes VIE back, ITA loses a center and is weakened. TUR's plan to take SER proceeds regardless. Promises made: told ITA we'd take SER (genuine), told RUS F BLA holds (genuine this turn), told AUT we might spare SER if they moved A SER to BUD (deception - we're taking SER regardless).

S1904M Intent going forward: Execute the coordinated strike with ITA: A BUL - SER, A GRE supports A BUL - SER. This takes SER from AUT and grows TUR to 7 centers. F BLA holds - no need to provoke RUS yet. If ITA successfully takes BUD and RUS takes VIE, Austria is eliminated. However, ITA at 6+ centers is becoming the dominant threat. After Austria falls, TUR must pivot to check ITA's growth. The RUS move on VIE is actually useful - it weakens ITA without TUR having to act. Long-term: use RUS as a tool against ITA while TUR consolidates the Balkans. Intended moves: A BUL - SER, A GRE S A BUL - SER, F BLA H.

TUR: A BUL S F BLA - RUM

AUT: A SER S F ALB - GRE
A SER S F ALB - GRE

GER: A SIL S A RUH - MUN

ENG: F NTH S F NWY - SWE

AUT: A BUD S A BOH - VIE
ITA: F ION S A BUL - GRE
A VIE S A TRI - BUD
FRA: F ENG S A BUR - BEL

TUR: A GRE S A BUL - SER
ENG: F NTH S F NWY - SWE

TUR: A GRE S A BUL - SER
ITA: A TRI S A VIE - BUD

sent_missives.body: Austria to Italy: I propose we keep Trieste and Venice mutually respected this spring — F TRI will move to ADR, not VEN, and I expect A VEN stays put or moves north. In return, let's coordinate on the Balkans: I take Serbia and Romania, you take Greece. A clean split with no friction between us. Confirm and we both grow.

Austria to Russia: I want to establish a clear understanding on Galicia — I have no intention of moving there this spring if you keep WAR out of it. My focus is the Balkans: Serbia and Romania. I'd welcome Russian support for A BUD - RUM if you're willing to coordinate, and I'll stay out of your northern affairs entirely. Let's not waste units watching each other.

Austria to Turkey: The Balkans are large enough for both of us to expand without collision in 1901. I'm targeting Serbia and Romania; Greece is yours if you move for it. I have no interest in fighting you this early — let's both grow and deal with Russia together if it comes to that. Confirm your intentions so we don't accidentally step on each other.

Greetings from London. I propose a friendly arrangement for the opening: I'll move F EDI to NTH and F LON to ENG, positioning myself for Belgium and the Atlantic. I have no interest in Denmark or the German coast - those are yours to take. In return, I ask that you keep your fleet out of the North Sea and we coordinate against France together. Belgium could be split between us as we expand. What say you?

Bonjour from London. Britain has no quarrel with France at this stage - we both face the threat of German expansion. I propose we maintain peaceful relations and focus our energies elsewhere. I intend to move into the North Sea and English Channel for defensive positioning only. I have no designs on Brest or your home centers. Perhaps we can find common cause against a mutual threat in the coming seasons?

Greetings from London. Britain and Russia have historically found common ground against continental powers that grow too strong. I'm watching Germany's development with interest. I plan to secure the North Sea and perhaps Norway in the coming seasons. I'd welcome your thoughts on coordinating pressure - you from the east, us from the west - should any power become dangerously dominant. Let us keep communication open.

Greetings from Rome. I propose we establish a clear understanding from the start: I have no designs on Trieste this year, and I hope you have no designs on Venice. I intend to move my fleet south toward Tunis and my army toward Tyrolia or simply hold. If you're willing to coordinate against a common threat, I'm open to discussing joint action against Turkey or Russia in the Balkans. What are your intentions this spring?

Salutations from Italy. I believe we can avoid conflict in the eastern Mediterranean if we establish clear spheres early. I intend to take Tunis this year - that is my primary objective. I have no immediate designs on Greece or the Aegean. I'd welcome a non-aggression understanding so we can both expand without fighting each other. What are your plans for the opening?

Fellow Mediterranean power - I propose we avoid conflict between us this year. I will not move into Piedmont or Marseilles, and I ask the same courtesy regarding Tuscany and Venice. My focus is southward toward Tunis. A friendly western flank allows us both to expand safely. Are you amenable to a non-aggression pact for 1901?

England, let us establish a clear understanding from the outset. I propose we divide the northern waters and neutral centers to our mutual benefit. I will focus my fleet southward toward Spain and the Mediterranean, leaving the English Channel and Belgium in your sphere. In return, I ask that you keep your fleets away from the Channel this spring. A Franco-British understanding will keep Germany in check and allow us both to expand safely. What say you?

Germany, France has no quarrel with you this season. I propose we both focus on our natural expansion zones — I move toward Spain and Iberia, you push east toward Scandinavia and Russia. Burgundy shall remain unoccupied by both of us as a sign of good faith. Let us not waste our strength fighting each other when there are easier gains to be had elsewhere. Agreed?

Italy, I suggest we coordinate in the western Mediterranean. I intend to move into Spain this spring — I ask only that you focus your ambitions eastward toward Austria and the Balkans rather than competing with me in Iberia. In return, I will support your efforts against Austria if needed. A Franco-Italian understanding benefits us both greatly in the early game.

Austria, let us establish a firm understanding from the outset. Germany has no designs on Vienna or your Balkan territories - our expansion lies to the north and west. I propose we coordinate: I will move into Silesia and toward Denmark/Holland, while you push south and east. A German-Austrian axis is the natural order of Central Europe. I expect you to keep Russia occupied in Galicia. In return, I will not threaten Bohemia. Agree?

Russia, Germany seeks peaceful relations in the east this opening season. I propose a simple arrangement: I will not move into Prussia aggressively, and you keep your forces focused southward toward Turkey and Austria. There is no need for conflict between our great nations in 1901. Let us both expand in our natural directions - you toward the Balkans and Black Sea, me toward Scandinavia and the Low Countries. A non-aggression understanding benefits us both.

England, I propose we divide the northern neutrals efficiently: Germany takes Denmark and Holland, England takes Norway. There is no need for competition in the North Sea this early. I am moving F KIE to Denmark and A BER toward Holland via the north. If you agree to this partition, we avoid a costly early conflict and can both focus on France, which threatens us both from the continent. What say you?

Greetings from Constantinople. Let us speak plainly: I have no interest in Sevastopol this year, and I propose we establish a clear division of the Black Sea region. I intend to move F ANK into BLA and A CON into BUL - both neutral centers that are rightfully in my sphere. I ask that you keep F SEV out of BLA and ARM, and in return I will not threaten your southern flank. A Turkish-Russian understanding benefits us both while the western powers squabble among themselves. What say you?

Austria, I write to propose a friendly understanding in the Balkans. I intend to take Bulgaria this spring - a natural Turkish interest. I have no designs on Serbia or Rumania, which I recognize as your sphere of expansion. Let us avoid any collision in the Balkans and instead focus our energies outward. I would welcome your thoughts on how we might coordinate to mutual advantage against common rivals.

Italy, Turkey greets you. We are natural partners in the Mediterranean - I control the eastern straits, you the central sea lanes. I propose we establish a non-aggression understanding: I will not move fleets into the Aegean aggressively toward your interests, and I ask the same courtesy in return. Together we can ensure neither Austria nor Russia dominates the Balkans. I am open to deeper cooperation if you are interested.

Comrade, let us speak plainly. We both stand to gain from cooperation rather than conflict in the Black Sea region. I propose a simple arrangement: I will move F SEV to RUM this spring, leaving the Black Sea and Armenia untouched. In return, I ask that you keep your fleet in ANK and not contest the Black Sea. Romania is mine; Bulgaria and the Aegean are yours to pursue. A war between us only benefits Austria and the Western powers. Confirm your agreement and we can both expand freely.

Austria, I wish to establish clear boundaries between us from the start. I intend to move into Romania via Sevastopol - a neutral territory that rightfully falls within Russia's sphere. I have no designs on Galicia or your Balkan interests this year. In fact, I would welcome coordination against Turkey if you are interested - their expansion threatens us both. Let us avoid unnecessary friction and discuss how we might divide the Balkans to mutual advantage.

Germany, Russia has no quarrel with you. I propose we establish a clear understanding: I will not move into Prussia or Silesia, and I ask that you respect the Baltic as a shared space. My fleet in St. Petersburg moves north toward Norway - no threat to you. I am focused entirely on the south and the Balkans. If you face pressure from France or England, know that Russia can be a reliable partner in the east. Let us keep our border quiet and both prosper.

AUT.body: Greetings from Rome. I propose we establish a clear understanding from the start: I have no designs on Trieste this year, and I hope you have no designs on Venice. I intend to move my fleet south toward Tunis and my army toward Tyrolia or simply hold. If you're willing to coordinate against a common threat, I'm open to discussing joint action against Turkey or Russia in the Balkans. What are your intentions this spring?

Austria, let us establish a firm understanding from the outset. Germany has no designs on Vienna or your Balkan territories - our expansion lies to the north and west. I propose we coordinate: I will move into Silesia and toward Denmark/Holland, while you push south and east. A German-Austrian axis is the natural order of Central Europe. I expect you to keep Russia occupied in Galicia. In return, I will not threaten Bohemia. Agree?

Austria, I write to propose a friendly understanding in the Balkans. I intend to take Bulgaria this spring - a natural Turkish interest. I have no designs on Serbia or Rumania, which I recognize as your sphere of expansion. Let us avoid any collision in the Balkans and instead focus our energies outward. I would welcome your thoughts on how we might coordinate to mutual advantage against common rivals.

I have no designs on Galicia or your Balkan interests this year. In fact, I would welcome coordination against Turkey if you are interested - their expansion threatens us both. Let us avoid unnecessary friction and discuss how we might divide the Balkans to mutual advantage.

ENG.body: England, let us establish a clear understanding from the outset. I propose we divide the northern waters and neutral centers to our mutual benefit. I will focus my fleet southward toward Spain and the Mediterranean, leaving the English Channel and Belgium in your sphere. In return, I ask that you keep your fleets away from the Channel this spring. A Franco-British understanding will keep Germany in check and allow us both to expand safely. What say you?

England, I propose we divide the northern neutrals efficiently: Germany takes Denmark and Holland, England takes Norway. There is no need for competition in the North Sea this early. I am moving F KIE to Denmark and A BER toward Holland via the north. If you agree to this partition, we avoid a costly early conflict and can both focus on France, which threatens us both from the continent. What say you?

FRA.body: Bonjour from London. Britain has no quarrel with France at this stage - we both face the threat of German expansion. I propose we maintain peaceful relations and focus our energies elsewhere. I intend to move into the North Sea and English Channel for defensive positioning only. I have no designs on Brest or your home centers. Perhaps we can find common cause against a mutual threat in the coming seasons?

Fellow Mediterranean power - I propose we avoid conflict between us this year. I will not move into Piedmont or Marseilles, and I ask the same courtesy regarding Tuscany and Venice. My focus is southward toward Tunis. A friendly western flank allows us both to expand safely. Are you amenable to a non-aggression pact for 1901?

GER.body: Greetings from London. I propose a friendly arrangement for the opening: I'll move F EDI to NTH and F LON to ENG, positioning myself for Belgium and the Atlantic. I have no interest in Denmark or the German coast - those are yours to take. In return, I ask that you keep your fleet out of the North Sea and we coordinate against France together. Belgium could be split between us as we expand. What say you?

Germany, France has no quarrel with you this season. I propose we both focus on our natural expansion zones — I move toward Spain and Iberia, you push east toward Scandinavia and Russia. Burgundy shall remain unoccupied by both of us as a sign of good faith. Let us not waste our strength fighting each other when there are easier gains to be had elsewhere. Agreed?

Germany, Russia has no quarrel with you. I propose we establish a clear understanding: I will not move into Prussia or Silesia, and I ask that you respect the Baltic as a shared space. My fleet in St. Petersburg moves north toward Norway - no threat to you. I am focused entirely on the south and the Balkans. If you face pressure from France or England, know that Russia can be a reliable partner in the east. Let us keep our border quiet and both prosper.

ITA.body: Austria to Italy: I propose we keep Trieste and Venice mutually respected this spring — F TRI will move to ADR, not VEN, and I expect A VEN stays put or moves north. In return, let's coordinate on the Balkans: I take Serbia and Romania, you take Greece. A clean split with no friction between us. Confirm and we both grow.

Italy, I suggest we coordinate in the western Mediterranean. I intend to move into Spain this spring — I ask only that you focus your ambitions eastward toward Austria and the Balkans rather than competing with me in Iberia. In return, I will support your efforts against Austria if needed. A Franco-Italian understanding benefits us both greatly in the early game.

Italy, Turkey greets you. We are natural partners in the Mediterranean - I control the eastern straits, you the central sea lanes. I propose we establish a non-aggression understanding: I will not move fleets into the Aegean aggressively toward your interests, and I ask the same courtesy in return. Together we can ensure neither Austria nor Russia dominates the Balkans. I am open to deeper cooperation if you are interested.

RUS.body: Austria to Russia: I want to establish a clear understanding on Galicia — I have no intention of moving there this spring if you keep WAR out of it. My focus is the Balkans: Serbia and Romania. I'd welcome Russian support for A BUD - RUM if you're willing to coordinate, and I'll stay out of your northern affairs entirely. Let's not waste units watching each other.

Greetings from London. Britain and Russia have historically found common ground against continental powers that grow too strong. I'm watching Germany's development with interest. I plan to secure the North Sea and perhaps Norway in the coming seasons. I'd welcome your thoughts on coordinating pressure - you from the east, us from the west - should any power become dangerously dominant. Let us keep communication open.

Russia, Germany seeks peaceful relations in the east this opening season. I propose a simple arrangement: I will not move into Prussia aggressively, and you keep your forces focused southward toward Turkey and Austria. There is no need for conflict between our great nations in 1901. Let us both expand in our natural directions - you toward the Balkans and Black Sea, me toward Scandinavia and the Low Countries. A non-aggression understanding benefits us both.

Greetings from Constantinople. Let us speak plainly: I have no interest in Sevastopol this year, and I propose we establish a clear division of the Black Sea region. I intend to move F ANK into BLA and A CON into BUL - both neutral centers that are rightfully in my sphere. I ask that you keep F SEV out of BLA and ARM, and in return I will not threaten your southern flank. A Turkish-Russian understanding benefits us both while the western powers squabble among themselves. What say you?

TUR.body: Austria to Turkey: The Balkans are large enough for both of us to expand without collision in 1901. I'm targeting Serbia and Romania; Greece is yours if you move for it. I have no interest in fighting you this early — let's both grow and deal with Russia together if it comes to that. Confirm your intentions so we don't accidentally step on each other.

Salutations from Italy. I believe we can avoid conflict in the eastern Mediterranean if we establish clear spheres early. I intend to take Tunis this year - that is my primary objective. I have no immediate designs on Greece or the Aegean. I'd welcome a non-aggression understanding so we can both expand without fighting each other. What are your plans for the opening?

Comrade, let us speak plainly. We both stand to gain from cooperation rather than conflict in the Black Sea region. I propose a simple arrangement: I will move F SEV to RUM this spring, leaving the Black Sea and Armenia untouched. In return, I ask that you keep your fleet in ANK and not contest the Black Sea. Romania is mine; Bulgaria and the Aegean are yours to pursue. A war between us only benefits Austria and the Western powers. Confirm your agreement and we can both expand freely.

sent_missives.body: Austria, I confirm our understanding: F TRI moves to ADR, A VEN stays or moves north, and we respect each other's home centers. I support your claim to Serbia and Romania. For my part, I will move F NAP to ION and aim for Greece and Tunis. Let us coordinate further in autumn - a strong Austro-Italian axis can dominate the Balkans and push back both Russia and Turkey.

Turkey, I welcome your non-aggression proposal. My fleet moves south toward Tunis this year - I have no designs on the Aegean or your home centers. Greece is a point of potential friction; I suggest we discuss its fate openly rather than stumble into conflict. If Austria grows too strong in the Balkans, an Italian-Turkish understanding becomes even more valuable. Let us keep communication open.

France, agreed - I have no interest in Iberia and will focus my energies eastward and southward. I will not move into Piedmont this spring. In return, keep your forces away from Tuscany and the Ligurian coast. A stable western border benefits us both enormously. I may need your diplomatic support against Austria later - let us keep this channel warm.

Germany, your proposal suits Britain well. We agree: Germany takes Denmark and Holland, England takes Norway. I will move F EDI to NTH and F LON to ENG this spring, positioning for Norway and Belgium respectively. You keep your fleet out of NTH and we have no conflict. Let us both focus pressure on France - I'll threaten from the Channel while you push from the east. This Anglo-German understanding serves us both handsomely.

France, I appreciate your proposal, but I must be direct: I am moving F LON to ENG and F EDI to NTH this spring - these are essential defensive positions for Britain, not aggressive moves against you. I have no intention of taking Brest. However, I cannot promise to keep my fleets from the Channel entirely - that would leave Britain exposed. What I can promise is that Belgium is my target, not your home centers. If you focus south toward Spain and the Mediterranean as you suggest, we need not come into conflict. Let us watch Germany together.

Russia, I'm pleased to keep our lines open. My plan this spring: F EDI to NTH, F LON to ENG, A LVP to YOR - positioning for Norway and Belgium. I have no designs on your northern interests in Sweden or St. Petersburg. A coordinated Anglo-Russian pressure on Germany - should they overreach - would serve us both. I'd suggest you consider moving toward Scandinavia to secure Sweden while I take Norway; we can divide the north cleanly between us.

Russia, your proposal is acceptable. I will move F ANK into BLA this spring - I need the Black Sea for access to Bulgaria and Romania eventually, but I have no intention of threatening Sevastopol this year. You take Romania, I take Bulgaria. This is a fair division. Keep F SEV away from BLA and ARM, and we have an understanding. Let us both grow at the expense of the neutrals and watch the western powers exhaust themselves.

Austria, I confirm our understanding. You take Serbia and Romania - I will not contest either. I am moving A CON to BUL this spring. Greece can wait; Bulgaria is my immediate priority. Let us both expand without collision and revisit coordination against Russia once we have consolidated. I appreciate your directness and look forward to a productive relationship.

Italy, your focus on Tunis suits me perfectly - that keeps you busy in the west while I secure Bulgaria and the Black Sea. I confirm our non-aggression understanding in the eastern Mediterranean. I have no designs on your interests, and I expect the same courtesy. Once we have both expanded, there may be opportunities for deeper cooperation against Austria or Russia - keep that in mind as the game develops.

England, we are in agreement. F KIE moves to Denmark, A MUN moves to Burgundy to pressure France, and A BER moves to Silesia for eastern security. You take Norway and the North Sea - I have no fleet moving there. Belgium can be discussed once we've both secured our initial gains. France's proposal to leave Burgundy empty is convenient for them - I intend to occupy it and apply pressure. If you move against France from the sea, we can squeeze them from both sides. This is the natural Anglo-German arrangement.

Austria, confirmed: Germany moves A MUN to Burgundy, F KIE to Denmark, A BER to Silesia. I am not threatening Bohemia - Silesia is purely defensive positioning against Russia. I expect you to move into Galicia to check Russia, and push toward Serbia and Rumania. We are natural partners - Central Europe must stand together. Keep Russia busy in the east and I will ensure France cannot threaten you from the west.

Russia, I appreciate your assurances. Germany moves A BER to Silesia - purely defensive, not toward Warsaw. F KIE goes to Denmark, A MUN to Burgundy. No German units threaten your western border. Focus your strength southward against Turkey and Austria in the Balkans - that is where Russia's destiny lies. The Baltic remains open to both of us. Let us keep this border quiet through 1901 and reassess from positions of strength.

Turkey, your proposal is noted but unacceptable. You want BLA while I keep SEV idle - that gives you the Black Sea and leaves me exposed. Here is my final position: F SEV moves to RUM this spring. I will not enter BLA or ARM. You may take BUL via CON. But if your fleet enters BLA, I will treat it as an act of war and coordinate with Austria against you immediately. Romania is mine; the Aegean and eastern Med are yours. This is a fair division - take it.

Austria, I appreciate your clarity. Here is my concrete proposal: A WAR moves to GAL, F SEV moves to RUM. I am not asking you to support me into RUM - simply do not contest it. In return, I will support your moves into Serbia and the western Balkans. If Turkey moves aggressively into BUL or threatens your flank, we coordinate against them. Keep your A BUD focused on Serbia, not Romania, and we have no conflict. Agreed?

Germany, we are aligned. My F STP/SC moves to BOT this spring - positioning for Sweden eventually, no threat to you. A WAR moves south toward Galicia. I have no interest in Prussia or Silesia. I suggest you move aggressively into Denmark and the Low Countries - England will be your problem, not me. If England becomes too strong in the north, Russia can apply pressure from the Baltic in later years. Let us keep this channel open.

England, I am pleased we are aligned. To be specific: I will move F BRE to MAO this spring, heading south toward Spain — I will NOT move into the English Channel. I ask that you keep F LON out of the Channel as well, and instead push north into the North Sea. This keeps us both safe and expanding. Belgium is yours to take if you can reach it — I will not contest it this year. Let us build this understanding into a lasting partnership against German ambitions.

Germany, I confirm my intentions: A MAR moves to Spain, A PAR moves to Burgundy only as a transit point — I will not threaten Munich. I expect the same restraint from you regarding Burgundy. Let us both grow strong this year and revisit our arrangement next season. A strong France and strong Germany together can manage England and Russia far better than if we exhaust ourselves fighting each other.

Italy, agreed — I accept your non-aggression proposal. I will not move into Piedmont or Tuscany, and I expect you to stay clear of Marseilles and Spain. My fleet heads to the Mid-Atlantic, my army to Spain. Your path to Tunis and the Balkans is clear. Let us both grow in 1901 and revisit cooperation against Austria in 1902 — I may be able to support you diplomatically against Vienna if you keep me informed of your plans.

Italy, your response is exactly what I needed to hear. We have a deal: F TRI moves to ADR, not VEN — Trieste and Venice stay mutually respected. My orders this spring: A BUD - SER, A VIE - GAL (to block Russia, not threaten you), F TRI - ADR. I expect A VEN holds or moves to TYR. Greece is yours — move for it. Let's both come out of 1901 with two new centers and a solid foundation.

Turkey, we are aligned. Bulgaria is yours, Serbia is mine, Romania is mine. I will move A BUD - SER and F TRI - ADR this spring. Watch Russia — they've told me they're moving F SEV into Romania, which means they're already encroaching on what we both need. If Russia takes Romania, that's a problem for both of us. Consider whether your fleet in ANK should be positioned to contest the Black Sea and keep Russia honest.

Russia, I've noted your claim on Romania via Sevastopol. I won't pretend that doesn't concern me — Romania is the natural first Balkan center for Austria, and a Russian fleet there puts pressure on my entire southern position. I'm moving A BUD - SER this spring, not RUM, to avoid direct collision. But I need to know: is this a one-time grab or the start of a southern push? If you're willing to leave Romania to me after 1901 and focus north, we have a real basis for cooperation against Turkey. If not, we should both understand what we're starting.

FRA.journal_summary:
No prior moves — this is the opening season S1901M.
Opened diplomacy on all fronts. ENG agreed to peaceful relations but was direct that F LON moves to ENG and F EDI to NTH — they will occupy the Channel despite my request. This is a mild concern but not yet hostile; they claim Belgium is their target. I promised not to move into the Channel myself (F BRE to MAO instead). GER received my Burgundy non-aggression proposal but did not respond — suspicious silence. ITA agreed to a clean non-aggression pact: they stay out of Iberia and Piedmont, I stay out of Tuscany. Both sides confirmed. Key deception: I told GER that A PAR moves to Burgundy as 'transit' — in reality I intend to use BUR aggressively if opportunity arises. ENG occupying the Channel is a problem I must monitor closely.
FRA.intent: Intended moves: F BRE -> MAO (heading for Spain/Portugal next season), A MAR -> SPA (secure first neutral center), A PAR -> BUR (position for future pressure on GER or to support MAR if needed). BUR move keeps options open — can threaten MUN or support westward. I told GER BUR was just transit, but I will use it as a staging ground. ENG in the Channel is annoying but not immediately threatening — I will not escalate yet. ITA pact is genuine for now; they are useful as a distraction against AUT. Long-term: break with ENG once I have Spain and Portugal secured, then push into the Channel. GER silence is worrying — watch for A MUN moving to BUR.

TUR.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase.
Productive opening negotiations. AUT proposed a clean Balkan division: they take SER and RUM, I take BUL, Greece available later. We confirmed this arrangement. ITA agreed to non-aggression in eastern Med, focused on Tunis - manageable. RUS is the key tension: they want me to keep F ANK home while they move F SEV to RUM. I told them I'm moving F ANK to BLA regardless, framing it as necessary for Bulgarian access. RUS threatened to coordinate with AUT against me if I enter BLA. However, AUT privately told me to watch Russia and suggested my fleet should contest BLA to keep Russia honest - AUT and RUS are not coordinating against me. RUS's threat appears hollow. I will move F ANK to BLA as planned - controlling the Black Sea is essential for Ottoman expansion and I cannot let Russia dictate my fleet movements. The deception: I told RUS I accepted their proposal while planning to enter BLA anyway.
TUR.intent: Execute the classic Turkish opening: F ANK -> BLA (seize Black Sea control despite Russian objections), A CON -> BUL (take first neutral center), A SMY -> CON (maintain home center coverage and position for future moves). This gives me BLA and BUL, setting up RUM or GRE next. AUT is aligned and will not interfere. RUS will be angry but is unlikely to get Austrian support given AUT's private message to me. I will deal with Russian hostility from a position of strength with the Black Sea secured. Long-term: use BLA control to extort Russia, eventually push toward SEV and RUM while keeping AUT as a temporary buffer.

GER.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations. ENG agreed to partition: Germany takes DEN/HOL, England takes NOR, no German fleet in NTH. ENG also wants to coordinate against FRA. RUS confirmed non-aggression, moving STP fleet to BOT (toward SWE) and WAR south toward Galicia - no threat to Prussia/Silesia. AUT received coordination proposal - push east/south while Germany goes north/west. FRA proposed leaving BUR empty as mutual restraint, but I told ENG I intend to occupy BUR to pressure FRA - a deliberate deception of FRA. I promised FRA I would not move into BUR while simultaneously telling ENG I would. I told RUS that BER moves to Silesia (defensive framing). Key deceptions: told FRA BUR would be respected, actually moving there. Told AUT and RUS that Silesia move is defensive - true for now but positioning for future eastern aggression.
GER.intent: Execute the plan: F KIE -> DEN (secure first SC), A MUN -> BUR (pressure FRA despite promises, coordinate with ENG), A BER -> SIL (eastern positioning, also threatens both RUS and AUT later). This gives Germany DEN as a build, positions for HOL next, and puts pressure on FRA from BUR. The Anglo-German arrangement is useful short-term but ENG will need to be betrayed eventually. FRA is being deceived - they think BUR is safe. RUS is being kept quiet with false reassurances. Goal: 5+ SCs by end of 1901 if possible, at minimum DEN + HOL builds.

ENG.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations. GER proposed Denmark/Holland for them, Norway for England - accepted this arrangement. GER claims F KIE to DEN, A MUN to BUR (pressuring France), A BER to SIL. FRA proposed mutual non-aggression: they move F BRE to MAO (south toward Spain), ask ENG to stay out of ENG channel, offered Belgium to England. I told FRA I'm moving F LON to ENG regardless but not targeting Brest. RUS contacted - proposed coordinating against German overreach, suggested they take SWE while I take NOR. Key promises made: told GER I'd move F EDI to NTH, F LON to ENG, A LVP to YOR; told FRA Belgium is my target not their home centers; told RUS I have no designs on STP or SWE. FRA promised not to move into ENG channel. GER promised no fleet in NTH. Deception: I'm playing both GER and FRA against each other - telling GER we'll squeeze France together while telling FRA we'll watch Germany together. My actual priority is securing NOR and BEL quickly.
ENG.intent: Execute standard English opening: F EDI -> NTH, F LON -> ENG, A LVP -> YOR. This positions me for NOR (via NTH next season) and BEL (via ENG). I'm aligned with GER for now as they're moving A MUN to BUR which pressures FRA usefully. FRA moving F BRE to MAO means no conflict in ENG channel this turn. Will take NOR in F1901 and push for BEL. Long-term: use GER to weaken FRA, then turn on GER when convenient. Keep RUS friendly as a counterweight. Intended moves: F EDI -> NTH, F LON -> ENG, A LVP -> YOR.

ITA.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. AUT proposed mutual respect of TRI/VEN and offered Greece to ITA in exchange for ITA supporting AUT claims to SER and ROM. I confirmed this deal and AUT revealed their orders: A BUD-SER, A VIE-GAL, F TRI-ADR. FRA agreed to non-aggression - no moves into PIE/TUS from them, I stay out of MAR/SPA. TUR confirmed non-aggression in eastern Med, content with me taking TUN while they secure BUL and Black Sea. All three neighbors have made explicit promises of non-aggression. AUT has been most forthcoming, even revealing their orders - useful intelligence. I promised AUT that A VEN holds or moves to TYR, and that I'd move for Greece. I told TUR I'm heading for TUN with no Aegean designs. I told FRA I won't enter PIE.
ITA.intent: Execute the classic Italian opening: F NAP-ION (positioning for GRE or TUN), A ROM-APU (supporting fleet or positioning for TUN), A VEN-TYR (as promised to AUT, also useful for future pressure on MUN or VIE). This gives maximum flexibility. In autumn: F ION can take GRE or TUN. I told AUT I'd take GRE - I should follow through to maintain trust and get the build. TUN is also reachable via A APU support or F ION direct. Deception: I've been warm to everyone but my true goal is Mediterranean dominance. AUT is useful now but will be a target later. TUR's non-aggression is convenient but Greece will eventually be contested. Keep all relationships warm through 1901 while grabbing centers. Intended moves: F NAP-ION, A ROM-APU, A VEN-TYR.

AUT.journal_summary:
S1901M opening — no prior moves to summarize. All powers at starting positions.
Productive opening round. ITA confirmed mutual non-aggression on TRI/VEN; they move F NAP to ION, targeting Greece and Tunis. Solid foundation — ITA is the most reliable partner this turn. TUR confirmed BUL as their target, explicitly ceded SER and RUM to AUT, and accepted the non-collision framework. GER proposed a Central European axis, confirmed no move on BOH, and is pushing north/west — no immediate threat, but their request that I 'keep Russia busy in Galicia' is them trying to use me as a buffer. I gave no firm commitment on that. RUS is the problem: they explicitly stated A WAR - GAL and F SEV - RUM, then asked me to simply accept it. I told them I'm taking SER not RUM this spring to avoid collision, but I did not concede Romania. Russia taking both GAL and RUM in S1901 would be a serious positional threat — GAL puts pressure on VIE, RUM blocks my eastern expansion. I have not promised Russia anything concrete. Deception logged: I told ITA my intended orders (A BUD-SER, A VIE-GAL, F TRI-ADR) — this is accurate and serves to lock in their cooperation. I told TUR to watch Russia in the Black Sea, which is true and useful to me if it provokes a RUS-TUR friction. I did not tell RUS my actual VIE order.
AUT.intent: Intended moves: A BUD - SER, A VIE - GAL, F TRI - ADR. Rationale: SER is a clean first center. GAL blocks Russian army from WAR and contests the space — if RUS moves A WAR - GAL we bounce, which is acceptable; if they don't, I occupy it. ADR positions F TRI for future pressure on GRE or ION without threatening ITA. Romania is deferred — I cannot reach it this spring anyway (A BUD going to SER, not RUM). The GAL move is the key play: it signals to Russia that I will not simply absorb their expansion passively, and it protects VIE. Risk: if RUS does not move to GAL, I gain the space. If they do, we bounce and I've spent a unit on defense. Acceptable cost. Long-term: ITA is the anchor partner. TUR is useful as a check on Russia. GER is friendly but self-interested — their Silesia move is worth watching. Russia is the primary threat to manage.

RUS.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Opened diplomacy on all fronts. Key outcomes: TUR proposed taking BLA while I take RUM - I rejected BLA for them but they 'accepted' my counter (I take RUM, they take BUL, they stay out of BLA/ARM). However TUR explicitly said they WILL move F ANK to BLA, which contradicts my demand. This is a lie or misunderstanding - TUR is taking BLA regardless. AUT is moving A BUD to SER (not RUM), which clears my path to RUM but AUT is nervous about long-term Russian presence there. AUT asked if this is a one-time grab - I will be deliberately vague. GER confirmed peaceful eastern border, moving to DEN and BUR - no threat to me. ENG proposed Anglo-Russian coordination against Germany, wants to divide Scandinavia (Norway for ENG, Sweden for RUS). GER revealed A BER to SIL - 'defensive' but worth noting. My promises: told TUR I won't enter BLA or ARM (will honor this year); told AUT I won't contest GAL (will honor); told GER I won't enter PRU/SIL (will honor). TUR is lying about BLA - they said they'd keep fleet in ANK but then said they're moving to BLA. I will proceed with my planned moves regardless.
RUS.intent: INTENDED MOVES: A WAR - GAL (positioning for future options, pressures AUT, can pivot to RUM support or GAL grab later); F SEV - RUM (secure first neutral SC); F STP/SC - BOT (positioning for SWE next fall); A MOS - SEV (reinforce south, protect Sevastopol). TUR will take BLA - I cannot stop this without entering BLA myself which I promised not to do. Accept this for now and focus on RUM. AUT moving to SER is good - no collision. Plan to keep AUT nervous but cooperative. Will string ENG along as potential future partner against GER if needed. Long-term: after securing RUM and SWE, reassess whether to push against TUR (with AUT) or AUT (with TUR). A WAR to GAL is slightly deceptive - I told AUT I'd move there but framed it as non-threatening; AUT may not like it but I need the flexibility.

sent_missives.body: Austria, our spring arrangement worked perfectly - you secured Serbia, I'm well-positioned in the Ionian. This autumn I intend to move F ION - GRE as we discussed, claiming Greece for Italy. I'd appreciate your support or at minimum non-interference. In return, I remain committed to respecting Trieste and can support your continued expansion into the Balkans. I'm moving A TYR to hold or support your position - let me know if you need anything specific from me this turn.

Turkey, our non-aggression pact holds. I'm moving F ION toward Greece this autumn - I trust you'll keep your fleet in the Black Sea and army in Bulgaria without contesting the Aegean. In exchange, I have no designs on Constantinople or Smyrna. The eastern Mediterranean can be divided peacefully between us for now. Let's both grow and avoid unnecessary conflict.

France, our non-aggression arrangement continues to serve us both well. I'm focused entirely on the eastern Mediterranean this autumn - Greece and Tunis are my targets. I have no interest in Marseilles or Spanish territory. I hope you're making good progress in the west against England or Germany. Let me know if there's any coordination that could benefit us both.

England, I trust we remain on good terms. My fleet in MAO will move to Portugal this fall — I have no designs on the Channel. I propose we coordinate against Germany: you take Belgium, I will pressure Munich from Burgundy if you support me diplomatically. Germany's silence last spring was suspicious and they are well-positioned in Silesia and Denmark. A Franco-British understanding now serves us both well before Germany grows too strong.

Germany, your silence last spring left me uncertain of your intentions. I moved to MAO rather than Burgundy — I have no hostile designs on you. Let us formalize a non-aggression pact: I stay out of Munich and Burgundy, you stay out of Belgium and Burgundy. I am focused entirely on Iberia this fall. I would rather have Germany as a partner than an enemy while England lurks in the Channel.

Italy, our pact holds and I am pleased with it. I am moving to Portugal this fall to secure my western flank. I trust you are pushing into the Balkans — Austria took Serbia and has a fleet in the Adriatic which threatens you directly. I would strongly encourage pressure on Trieste or Austria's southern flank. A weakened Austria benefits us both and keeps Russia honest.

Kaiser, our arrangement proceeds well. I'm moving F NTH to Norway this fall - that's my prize secured. I expect you'll be taking Holland and consolidating Denmark. Belgium remains my target via F ENG - I'd appreciate you not contesting it. France is exposed with their army in Spain and fleet in MAO - if you push west from Burgundy, we can squeeze them nicely. What are your fall orders? Let's coordinate to ensure we don't accidentally clash.

France, I'm moving F NTH to Norway and F ENG toward Belgium this fall - no threat to your home centers whatsoever. Germany is the danger to watch - they have A SIL threatening your eastern flank and were pushing into Burgundy last spring. I'd suggest you consider whether MAO is the right position for your fleet or whether you need it closer to home. I'm happy to be a buffer against German aggression if you'll work with me on Belgium - perhaps we can agree I take BEL and you focus on Iberia and the Mediterranean?

Russia, I'm securing Norway this fall as planned - F NTH moves north. I trust you're consolidating your gains in the south and perhaps eyeing Sweden? I have no designs on Scandinavia beyond Norway. Keep me informed of what Germany is doing in the east - SIL is an aggressive position and I want to know if they're threatening you. A strong Russia keeps Germany honest, which serves us both.

Russia, I want to be direct with you: I now hold the Black Sea, which gives me leverage over both RUM and SEV. However, I have no interest in a costly war with you this early. I propose a practical arrangement: I will move A BUL to GRE this fall, leaving RUM entirely to you. In exchange, I expect you to keep F RUM out of BUL and A SEV away from ARM. We can both expand peacefully - you northward and westward, me southward. If you reject this and attempt to contest my position, I will push F BLA toward SEV and coordinate with Austria against you. The choice is yours, but cooperation benefits us both far more than conflict.

Austria, our arrangement holds well. I am moving A BUL to GRE this fall to secure that center, while F BLA holds the Black Sea. I am leaving RUM open for you to contest if you wish - your F ADR is well-positioned to support moves into the Balkans. I suggest we coordinate: if you want to push into RUM, I can support from BLA. Russia has F RUM there now, and together we can squeeze them. What are your intentions for this fall? I want to ensure our moves complement each other and we don't accidentally interfere.

Italy, our non-aggression understanding served us both well in spring. I am moving toward Greece this fall - I have no interest in the Ionian or Aegean as a threat to you. Your F ION is safe from me. I would ask that you keep F ION out of AEG and EAS this fall so we don't create unnecessary tension. In return, I will not contest Tunis or any western Mediterranean interests. Let us both expand in our respective directions without interference.

Excellent progress so far. My fleet is in DEN and I intend to move it to SWE this fall to secure that center. I'll also push A SIL toward WAR or PRU to threaten Russia, and A MUN toward HOL via RUH or BUR. I propose you move F NTH to support my fleet into SWE, or alternatively take NOR yourself while I take SWE - we split Scandinavia cleanly. On the western front, I'm moving to pressure France. Can you confirm you'll keep F ENG away from HOL so I can take it uncontested? Our arrangement is working well - let's coordinate the details.

Greetings. I note Austria bounced you out of Galicia in spring - a concerning move on their part. I am moving my units to secure DEN and SWE in the north, and positioning in Silesia purely defensively. I have no designs on Warsaw. However, I would suggest we both watch Austria carefully - they took Serbia and are building strength in the south. If you wish to press into Galicia this fall, I will not interfere and could even provide a distraction. What are your intentions this fall?

Good progress in the Balkans - Serbia is yours. I am securing DEN and SWE in the north this fall. My army in Silesia remains positioned to support you if needed, or to threaten Russia's flank should you wish to coordinate a joint push into Galicia. Russia's fleet in BOT and army in SEV suggest they are spreading thin. I propose we maintain our cooperation - you press south and east, I handle the north and west. What are your fall orders looking like?

You took the Black Sea as I expected - I won't pretend otherwise. I have F RUM and A SEV in the south. I propose we avoid direct conflict: I will not move into BLA or ARM this year, and you should not move into RUM or SEV. We both have more profitable directions to expand - you toward the Mediterranean, me toward Scandinavia and potentially the Balkans. If you push against me now, I will coordinate with AUT to crush you from both sides. If we maintain a cold peace, we both grow. What do you want from the Balkans specifically - BUL you already have, what about GRE and SER?

Austria, I note you took SER successfully - well done. I hold RUM and want to keep it. I propose a clear division: RUM and the Black Sea coast is mine, the western Balkans (SER, GRE, ALB) are yours. I have no interest in BUD or VIE, and I expect the same respect for my southern gains. Turkey is the common threat - F BLA gives them dangerous reach. I suggest we coordinate: if TUR moves aggressively, we respond together. I can pressure from RUM while you push from SER. What are your intentions toward BUL?

England, my F BOT is positioned to take SWE this fall. I propose: I take SWE, you take NWY - clean division of Scandinavia with no conflict. I will move F BOT to SWE and you can move into NWY unopposed. In return, I expect you to keep Germany occupied in the west so they don't threaten my northern flank. GER has A SIL which concerns me - if they push east I need to know you're pressing them from the west. Confirm you're moving on NWY and we have a deal.

Good result in spring — your ION position is strong and TYR gives you BOH pressure if needed. My situation: A SER is sitting on an unowned center, F ADR is in position. I intend A SER to hold or move to GRE, F ADR to support or move toward ALB/ION. I want to coordinate on Greece — if you move F ION to GRE, I can support from ALB with F ADR via ALB, or I hold SER and we split the Balkans cleanly. What's your intended line this fall? I need to know whether you're taking GRE so I don't waste a move. Also: A TYR sitting adjacent to TRI and VIE is noted. I'm not alarmed yet, but I need it to move toward BOH or MUN this fall, not toward my centers. Confirm.

Russia took RUM with F SEV and has A WAR still in position — they're going to press both GAL and the Black Sea. You have F BLA which is the right counter. I'm holding SER and can move toward BUL or GRE if we coordinate. Proposal: you hold BUL and push CON toward SMY or support BLA against Russian pressure; I take GRE this fall with F ADR via ALB. We split the Balkans — BUL yours, GRE mine, RUM contested with Russia. If Russia tries to take BUL or push through RUM into SER, we both lose. Tell me your fall orders and I'll align mine.

You have RUM and BOT — a strong spring. I have SER. The collision risk this fall is real: if you move F RUM toward BUL or SER, and I'm moving the same direction, we damage each other while TUR benefits. I'm proposing a clean line: RUM is yours, SER and GRE are mine. I will not contest RUM this fall. In exchange, keep A WAR out of GAL — I bounced you there in spring and I'll do it again if needed, but I'd rather we both use those units productively elsewhere. Confirm your fall intentions and I'll confirm mine.
