sent_missives.body: England, agreed — you take Belgium, I focus on Iberia. My fleet moves to Portugal this fall, A SPA stays put to consolidate. I will move A PAR to Burgundy to signal pressure on Germany and keep them honest. Germany in Silesia with Denmark secured is a growing threat to both of us — if you take Belgium and I hold Burgundy, we have Germany squeezed from two sides. Let us build on this understanding into 1902.

Germany, I am moving to Portugal this fall — purely westward expansion. A PAR moves to Burgundy only as a defensive buffer against any English adventurism from Belgium. I have no designs on Munich. I propose we both stay out of each other's territory through 1902 and focus on our natural expansion zones. Your silence last spring made me nervous, but I am willing to trust you if you confirm this arrangement.

Italy, Greece and Tunis are excellent targets — I fully support your eastern push. Austria's fleet in the Adriatic is a direct threat to you; I urge you to consider moving A APU to support a push toward Trieste or Greece while your fleet covers the Ionian. A weakened Austria opens the Balkans for you and keeps Russia from dominating the east unchallenged. Our western and eastern expansions complement each other perfectly — let us stay coordinated.

Kaiser, confirmed: F NTH moves to Norway, F ENG moves to Belgium. I will NOT contest Holland - that's yours. On Sweden: Russia's F BOT is moving there this fall, so you may face a bounce if you try DEN-SWE. I'd suggest you consider whether to contest it or focus on Holland and the east. I'm keeping F ENG clear of HOL as promised. Let's both grow this fall and deal with France and Russia in 1902.

France, we are aligned. My F NTH goes to Norway, F ENG goes to Belgium - no threat to you whatsoever. Germany is moving A MUN toward Holland and has A SIL threatening Russia - they are expanding aggressively on all fronts. Your fleet in MAO going to Portugal is sensible for Iberian consolidation. I suggest you keep A SPA positioned to defend and consider whether A PAR should move toward Burgundy to check German ambitions. A Franco-British understanding is exactly what I want - Germany must not be allowed to dominate the continent.

Russia, confirmed - F NTH to Norway, you take Sweden with F BOT. Clean division, no conflict. Be aware Germany's F DEN may also attempt Sweden, so you may need to be decisive. I will keep pressure on Germany from the west - Belgium is my target this fall which will occupy German attention. Watch A SIL carefully; if they push toward Warsaw I want to know immediately so we can coordinate a response. Our arrangement stands.

Austria, confirmed: F ION moves to GRE this fall - that center is mine. A TYR will move to BOH as you requested, not toward your home centers. I need your F ADR to stay out of the Ionian and not interfere with my Greek move. If A SER moves to GRE we will clash - please hold SER or move it elsewhere. I'm honoring our arrangement and expect the same. Let's both build this winter and discuss coordinated expansion in 1902.

Turkey, I hear your request about AEG and EAS - I will keep F ION out of AEG this fall, moving instead to GRE directly from ION. This should not threaten you. However, I must be clear: Greece is Italy's target this autumn. If A BUL moves to GRE we will have a serious problem. Hold BUL and we remain at peace. I have no interest in your home centers and will honor our non-aggression fully if you respect my claim to Greece.

France, Austria's F ADR is a concern but I have it managed diplomatically for now - I'm taking Greece this fall and building. Austria is useful as a buffer against Russia and Turkey for the moment, but I agree they cannot be allowed to dominate the Balkans unchecked. Once I have Greece and Tunis secured, I'll be in position to pressure Austria's southern flank. Let's stay coordinated - keep me informed of your western progress and I'll do the same from the south.

Agreed - cold peace it is. I will keep F RUM stationary and A SEV out of ARM this fall. You move A BUL to GRE, I take SWE in the north. We both grow without bleeding each other. But understand this clearly: if F BLA moves toward SEV or RUM at any point, I will treat it as an act of war and coordinate with AUT immediately. Honor this arrangement and we can discuss longer-term cooperation against whoever grows too strong in the west.

Austria, we have a deal. RUM is mine, SER and GRE are yours - I will not contest them. A WAR will not move to GAL this fall; I am moving it north toward LVN to support my Scandinavian push. F RUM will hold to secure the center. TUR is moving A BUL to GRE which means they're expanding south - watch that carefully, as GRE borders your sphere. If TUR overextends, we should be ready to act together. For now, let us both consolidate and grow without collision.

England, confirmed - F BOT moves to SWE this fall, you take NWY. Clean split, no conflict. On Germany: A SIL is positioned to threaten either WAR or GAL. I am watching it carefully. If GER pushes east, I will need you pressing hard from the west - make sure they feel pressure in BUR and DEN. A Germany squeezed between us cannot threaten either of us. Let us stay coordinated and communicate regularly as the board develops.

Conflict on GRE: you say F ION - GRE, TUR says A BUL - GRE. One of you gets it, one gets bounced. I am not moving to GRE this fall — I'm taking ADR to ALB and holding SER. If you want GRE, you need to know TUR is going for it too. I will not support either of you into GRE; that's your fight. What I need from you is A TYR moving to BOH or MUN — not toward TRI or VIE. Confirm that move and our arrangement holds. If TYR stays adjacent to my centers, I treat it as a threat and respond accordingly.

ITA is moving F ION - GRE this fall. You will bounce if you move A BUL - GRE. I am not going to GRE — I'm moving F ADR to ALB and holding SER. If you want to avoid wasting A BUL on a bounce, consider holding BUL or moving toward CON to consolidate. On RUM: Russia has it and I've told them I won't contest it this fall. If you want to pressure RUM from BLA, that's your call — I won't interfere. Our Balkan split stands: BUL yours, SER mine, GRE contested between you and ITA.

Agreed on the division: RUM is yours, I will not move on it this fall. My orders: A SER holds, F ADR moves to ALB. I am not contesting your southern position. In exchange, keep A WAR out of GAL — I have A VIE positioned to bounce you again if needed, and that wastes both our turns. One flag: TUR has A BUL and F BLA — they are your more immediate problem in the south, not me. If BUL moves toward RUM, you'll want to respond. I can hold SER as a buffer if that's useful to you.

Confirmed - my orders this fall: F DEN -> SWE, A MUN -> RUH (positioning for HOL next spring), A SIL -> WAR or holds to threaten Russia. I will not contest Belgium - that is yours via F ENG. You take NOR, I take SWE, you take BEL, I take HOL next spring. France is squeezed from both sides. This is the arrangement that wins us both the game in the west. Do not let F NTH wander - I need SWE secured and your fleet in NOR keeps Russia honest in the north.

France, I accept your proposal of mutual non-aggression. I am moving to secure Scandinavia this fall - DEN fleet goes to SWE, armies repositioning northward. I have no orders directed at Burgundy or Belgium this fall. Focus on Iberia, I focus on the north - we have no quarrel. England in the Channel is the greater threat to both of us long-term. Let us keep this channel of communication open.

Russia, I am moving F DEN to SWE this fall to secure Scandinavia. My army in Silesia will hold or move to PRU - no threat to Warsaw, I give you my word. Austria's bounce of your Galicia move in spring was provocative - they are growing strong in the Balkans with Serbia taken and their fleet in the Adriatic. I would strongly suggest pressing into Galicia this fall while I keep Austria's attention divided. A coordinated Russo-German pressure on Austria benefits us both enormously.

Austria, I've reviewed your proposal carefully. Here are my confirmed fall orders: A BUL moves to GRE, F BLA holds in the Black Sea to maintain pressure on Russia. A CON will support BUL's move to GRE. This means GRE goes to me this fall - I need that center. You should take ALB with F ADR and position for future moves. On RUM: I can support you into RUM from BLA if you have a unit that can reach it, but I don't see how you get there this fall without a unit adjacent. Let's focus on what's achievable: you hold SER, I take GRE, and we both pressure Russia from our respective positions. Confirm your orders so we don't accidentally interfere.

Russia, I accept your cold peace proposal for now. My fall orders: A BUL to GRE, F BLA holds. I am not moving into RUM or SEV this fall - Greece is my target, not your territory. In return, keep F RUM out of BUL and A SEV away from ARM. We both grow in different directions this year. However, understand that F BLA remains in the Black Sea permanently - that is non-negotiable. If you respect my sphere, I will respect yours. Next year we can discuss a more formal arrangement regarding RUM and the Balkans.

Italy, I see we have a conflict: you are moving F ION toward Greece and I am moving A BUL toward Greece. One of us will be disappointed. I propose you redirect F ION toward Tunis instead - that is a guaranteed center for you with no competition, while Greece is my natural expansion. If we both move on Greece we both risk bouncing and gaining nothing. Take Tunis this fall, I take Greece, and we both end the year with gains. This is the rational outcome for both of us. Please reconsider your move.

AUT.body: Austria, confirmed: F ION moves to GRE this fall - that center is mine. A TYR will move to BOH as you requested, not toward your home centers. I need your F ADR to stay out of the Ionian and not interfere with my Greek move. If A SER moves to GRE we will clash - please hold SER or move it elsewhere. I'm honoring our arrangement and expect the same. Let's both build this winter and discuss coordinated expansion in 1902.

Austria, we have a deal. RUM is mine, SER and GRE are yours - I will not contest them. A WAR will not move to GAL this fall; I am moving it north toward LVN to support my Scandinavian push. F RUM will hold to secure the center. TUR is moving A BUL to GRE which means they're expanding south - watch that carefully, as GRE borders your sphere. If TUR overextends, we should be ready to act together. For now, let us both consolidate and grow without collision.

Austria, I've reviewed your proposal carefully. Here are my confirmed fall orders: A BUL moves to GRE, F BLA holds in the Black Sea to maintain pressure on Russia. A CON will support BUL's move to GRE. This means GRE goes to me this fall - I need that center. You should take ALB with F ADR and position for future moves. On RUM: I can support you into RUM from BLA if you have a unit that can reach it, but I don't see how you get there this fall without a unit adjacent. Let's focus on what's achievable: you hold SER, I take GRE, and we both pressure Russia from our respective positions. Confirm your orders so we don't accidentally interfere.

ENG.body: England, agreed — you take Belgium, I focus on Iberia. My fleet moves to Portugal this fall, A SPA stays put to consolidate. I will move A PAR to Burgundy to signal pressure on Germany and keep them honest. Germany in Silesia with Denmark secured is a growing threat to both of us — if you take Belgium and I hold Burgundy, we have Germany squeezed from two sides. Let us build on this understanding into 1902.

England, confirmed - F BOT moves to SWE this fall, you take NWY. Clean split, no conflict. On Germany: A SIL is positioned to threaten either WAR or GAL. I am watching it carefully. If GER pushes east, I will need you pressing hard from the west - make sure they feel pressure in BUR and DEN. A Germany squeezed between us cannot threaten either of us. Let us stay coordinated and communicate regularly as the board develops.

Confirmed - my orders this fall: F DEN -> SWE, A MUN -> RUH (positioning for HOL next spring), A SIL -> WAR or holds to threaten Russia. I will not contest Belgium - that is yours via F ENG. You take NOR, I take SWE, you take BEL, I take HOL next spring. France is squeezed from both sides. This is the arrangement that wins us both the game in the west. Do not let F NTH wander - I need SWE secured and your fleet in NOR keeps Russia honest in the north.

FRA.body: France, we are aligned. My F NTH goes to Norway, F ENG goes to Belgium - no threat to you whatsoever. Germany is moving A MUN toward Holland and has A SIL threatening Russia - they are expanding aggressively on all fronts. Your fleet in MAO going to Portugal is sensible for Iberian consolidation. I suggest you keep A SPA positioned to defend and consider whether A PAR should move toward Burgundy to check German ambitions. A Franco-British understanding is exactly what I want - Germany must not be allowed to dominate the continent.

France, Austria's F ADR is a concern but I have it managed diplomatically for now - I'm taking Greece this fall and building. Austria is useful as a buffer against Russia and Turkey for the moment, but I agree they cannot be allowed to dominate the Balkans unchecked. Once I have Greece and Tunis secured, I'll be in position to pressure Austria's southern flank. Let's stay coordinated - keep me informed of your western progress and I'll do the same from the south.

France, I accept your proposal of mutual non-aggression. I am moving to secure Scandinavia this fall - DEN fleet goes to SWE, armies repositioning northward. I have no orders directed at Burgundy or Belgium this fall. Focus on Iberia, I focus on the north - we have no quarrel. England in the Channel is the greater threat to both of us long-term. Let us keep this channel of communication open.

GER.body: Germany, I am moving to Portugal this fall — purely westward expansion. A PAR moves to Burgundy only as a defensive buffer against any English adventurism from Belgium. I have no designs on Munich. I propose we both stay out of each other's territory through 1902 and focus on our natural expansion zones. Your silence last spring made me nervous, but I am willing to trust you if you confirm this arrangement.

Kaiser, confirmed: F NTH moves to Norway, F ENG moves to Belgium. I will NOT contest Holland - that's yours. On Sweden: Russia's F BOT is moving there this fall, so you may face a bounce if you try DEN-SWE. I'd suggest you consider whether to contest it or focus on Holland and the east. I'm keeping F ENG clear of HOL as promised. Let's both grow this fall and deal with France and Russia in 1902.

ITA.body: Italy, Greece and Tunis are excellent targets — I fully support your eastern push. Austria's fleet in the Adriatic is a direct threat to you; I urge you to consider moving A APU to support a push toward Trieste or Greece while your fleet covers the Ionian. A weakened Austria opens the Balkans for you and keeps Russia from dominating the east unchallenged. Our western and eastern expansions complement each other perfectly — let us stay coordinated.

Conflict on GRE: you say F ION - GRE, TUR says A BUL - GRE. One of you gets it, one gets bounced. I am not moving to GRE this fall — I'm taking ADR to ALB and holding SER. If you want GRE, you need to know TUR is going for it too. I will not support either of you into GRE; that's your fight. What I need from you is A TYR moving to BOH or MUN — not toward TRI or VIE. Confirm that move and our arrangement holds. If TYR stays adjacent to my centers, I treat it as a threat and respond accordingly.

Italy, I see we have a conflict: you are moving F ION toward Greece and I am moving A BUL toward Greece. One of us will be disappointed. I propose you redirect F ION toward Tunis instead - that is a guaranteed center for you with no competition, while Greece is my natural expansion. If we both move on Greece we both risk bouncing and gaining nothing. Take Tunis this fall, I take Greece, and we both end the year with gains. This is the rational outcome for both of us. Please reconsider your move.

RUS.body: Russia, confirmed - F NTH to Norway, you take Sweden with F BOT. Clean division, no conflict. Be aware Germany's F DEN may also attempt Sweden, so you may need to be decisive. I will keep pressure on Germany from the west - Belgium is my target this fall which will occupy German attention. Watch A SIL carefully; if they push toward Warsaw I want to know immediately so we can coordinate a response. Our arrangement stands.

Agreed on the division: RUM is yours, I will not move on it this fall. My orders: A SER holds, F ADR moves to ALB. I am not contesting your southern position. In exchange, keep A WAR out of GAL — I have A VIE positioned to bounce you again if needed, and that wastes both our turns. One flag: TUR has A BUL and F BLA — they are your more immediate problem in the south, not me. If BUL moves toward RUM, you'll want to respond. I can hold SER as a buffer if that's useful to you.

Russia, I am moving F DEN to SWE this fall to secure Scandinavia. My army in Silesia will hold or move to PRU - no threat to Warsaw, I give you my word. Austria's bounce of your Galicia move in spring was provocative - they are growing strong in the Balkans with Serbia taken and their fleet in the Adriatic. I would strongly suggest pressing into Galicia this fall while I keep Austria's attention divided. A coordinated Russo-German pressure on Austria benefits us both enormously.

Russia, I accept your cold peace proposal for now. My fall orders: A BUL to GRE, F BLA holds. I am not moving into RUM or SEV this fall - Greece is my target, not your territory. In return, keep F RUM out of BUL and A SEV away from ARM. We both grow in different directions this year. However, understand that F BLA remains in the Black Sea permanently - that is non-negotiable. If you respect my sphere, I will respect yours. Next year we can discuss a more formal arrangement regarding RUM and the Balkans.

TUR.body: Turkey, I hear your request about AEG and EAS - I will keep F ION out of AEG this fall, moving instead to GRE directly from ION. This should not threaten you. However, I must be clear: Greece is Italy's target this autumn. If A BUL moves to GRE we will have a serious problem. Hold BUL and we remain at peace. I have no interest in your home centers and will honor our non-aggression fully if you respect my claim to Greece.

Agreed - cold peace it is. I will keep F RUM stationary and A SEV out of ARM this fall. You move A BUL to GRE, I take SWE in the north. We both grow without bleeding each other. But understand this clearly: if F BLA moves toward SEV or RUM at any point, I will treat it as an act of war and coordinate with AUT immediately. Honor this arrangement and we can discuss longer-term cooperation against whoever grows too strong in the west.

ITA is moving F ION - GRE this fall. You will bounce if you move A BUL - GRE. I am not going to GRE — I'm moving F ADR to ALB and holding SER. If you want to avoid wasting A BUL on a bounce, consider holding BUL or moving toward CON to consolidate. On RUM: Russia has it and I've told them I won't contest it this fall. If you want to pressure RUM from BLA, that's your call — I won't interfere. Our Balkan split stands: BUL yours, SER mine, GRE contested between you and ITA.

FRA.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 saw France execute a clean opening: BRE-MAO, MAR-SPA, PAR-BUR. However, GER also moved MUN-BUR, creating a bounce that left PAR still in Paris and BUR empty. GER secured DEN and SIL. ENG took NTH and ENG channel with YOR. AUT grabbed SER and ADR. RUS took RUM and BOT. TUR secured BLA and BUL. Italy pushed ION, APU, TYR. France ends spring with MAO and SPA secured, PAR back home — no new centers yet but well-positioned for Portugal.
ENG proposed alignment: they take BEL, I focus on Iberia. I agreed publicly, promising F MAO-POR, A SPA holds, A PAR-BUR as 'defensive buffer against Germany.' ENG confirmed F NTH-NOR, F ENG-BEL — no threat to France. GER accepted non-aggression, claims DEN fleet goes to SWE and armies north — no moves toward BUR or BEL. I told GER that PAR-BUR is defensive against England, not Germany — a useful deception. ITA confirmed Greece and Tunis as targets, treating Austria diplomatically for now but open to future pressure. ITA is not a threat to me. Key deceptions: I am playing ENG and GER against each other while positioning in BUR — BUR gives me leverage over both. I have no intention of honoring the non-aggression with GER long-term once I am strong enough.
FRA.intent: Intended moves: F MAO-POR (secure first new center), A SPA holds (consolidate), A PAR-BUR (pressure Germany, signal to England, gain strategic position). BUR is the key square — holding it threatens MUN and keeps GER nervous while satisfying ENG that I am checking Germany. In 1902 I will build a new unit and look to strike either GER or ENG depending on who is weaker. ENG taking BEL is fine for now — it keeps GER occupied. Long-term I want BEL, MUN, and eventually more. Italy remains a useful southern partner to keep AUT and RUS busy. I will maintain the ENG alliance only as long as it serves French expansion westward and northward.

TUR.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 went perfectly. F ANK moved to BLA securing the Black Sea, A CON moved to BUL taking that center, A SMY moved to CON as replacement. Russia took RUM with F SEV and has A SEV now in Sevastopol. Austria took SER and moved F TRI to ADR. Italy moved aggressively with F NAP to ION, A ROM to APU, A VEN to TYR - clearly positioning for Greece and possibly more. Germany took DEN, bounced with France in BUR. England took NTH and ENG.
Key developments: ITA is moving F ION directly to GRE this fall - confirmed in both rounds. If I move A BUL to GRE we bounce and both gain nothing. AUT confirmed they are NOT going to GRE, moving F ADR to ALB instead and holding SER. RUS accepted cold peace: they keep F RUM stationary and A SEV out of ARM, I keep F BLA in Black Sea and don't touch RUM/SEV. I promised RUS I would move A BUL to GRE and hold F BLA - but now I know GRE is contested with ITA. I told AUT I was taking GRE with A BUL supported by A CON. Critical decision: if I move BUL-GRE and ITA moves ION-GRE, we bounce. I should consider moving A BUL to SER instead (AUT holds SER with A SER - that would bounce too) or holding BUL, or moving toward RUM. Actually best move: F BLA to RUM (Russia promised not to move F RUM, so RUM may be undefended or I can take it), A BUL holds or moves to GRE hoping ITA redirects. Wait - RUS said F RUM is stationary. If I move F BLA to RUM I break my promise to RUS but gain a center. This is the Ottoman way - use the cold peace to lull Russia then strike. A BUL to GRE still risks bounce with ITA. Better: F BLA - RUM (stab Russia, take center), A BUL holds (secure), A CON holds. This gives me RUM if Russia honored their word and kept F RUM stationary.
TUR.intent: Execute a calculated betrayal of Russia. Russia promised to keep F RUM stationary - I will exploit this by moving F BLA to RUM to capture that center. A BUL holds to secure Bulgaria. A CON holds to protect home center. This gives me a 4th center (RUM) and builds to 4 units. I told Russia I would hold F BLA and not touch RUM - this is a deliberate lie. I told AUT I was taking GRE - I will not, but AUT gains nothing from my deception here. ITA takes GRE uncontested. My moves: F BLA - RUM, A BUL holds, A CON holds. This secures RUM if Russia honored the cold peace agreement. Relationship with RUS will become hostile after this stab, but I will have 4 centers and can build a new fleet.

ENG.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 went smoothly for England. F EDI moved to NTH, F LON moved to ENG, A LVP moved to YOR - all successful. Germany took DEN with F KIE, moved A BER to SIL and A MUN to BUR (bounced with FRA's A PAR-BUR? Actually FRA moved A MAR-SPA and A PAR-BUR, GER moved A MUN-BUR - they bounced each other in BUR). Austria took SER and ADR. Russia moved to RUM, BOT, SEV, and WAR stayed. Turkey secured BLA and BUL. No builds for anyone as no centers changed hands yet.
Three-way coordination achieved this fall. GER confirmed: F DEN->SWE, A MUN->RUH, A SIL threatens east - and explicitly conceded BEL to ENG. RUS confirmed F BOT->SWE (will bounce GER in SWE - useful!), wants NOR for ENG. FRA confirmed F MAO->POR, A PAR->BUR to pressure GER, agreed ENG takes BEL. Key deceptions: I told GER that RUS is taking SWE so they may face a bounce - this is true and serves me by weakening GER's Scandinavian position. I'm playing both FRA and GER simultaneously - telling FRA that GER is the threat while telling GER we're squeezing FRA. I promised GER I'd keep F ENG away from HOL (keeping that promise this turn). I promised RUS I'd pressure GER from the west. I promised FRA a Franco-British understanding against GER. All three believe they have a special arrangement with me. The GER-RUS bounce in SWE benefits me - neither gains that center easily.
ENG.intent: Intended moves: F NTH -> NOR (securing Norway, +1 SC), F ENG -> BEL (securing Belgium, +1 SC), A YOR -> LVP (repositioning army back to home or hold). Actually A YOR should move somewhere useful - perhaps YOR holds or moves to support. With two builds coming, I'll be at 5 units next year. The GER-RUS SWE bounce is excellent - weakens both. I'm maintaining the illusion of alliance with GER, FRA, and RUS simultaneously. In 1902 I'll need to decide who to betray first - GER is the most dangerous continental power and FRA is a useful tool against them. RUS taking SWE would be fine as it keeps GER contained. Long-term: use FRA against GER, then turn on FRA once GER is weakened. Keep RUS as a distant partner for now.

ITA.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 went well for Italy. F NAP moved to ION, A ROM to APU, A VEN to TYR - all successful. Austria took Serbia and has F ADR in the Adriatic. Turkey secured BLA, BUL, CON. Russia grabbed RUM and BOT. No conflicts with Italy's moves.
Key developments: AUT confirmed they won't contest GRE but wants A TYR to move BOH, not toward their centers - I agreed. Critical intel: AUT revealed TUR is also moving A BUL to GRE, meaning F ION-GRE and A BUL-GRE will bounce. TUR confirmed this and proposed I take Tunis instead while they take Greece. I told TUR I'm still going for GRE and warned them off BUL. FRA is pushing me to pressure Austria but I'm not ready for that yet. The GRE situation is a genuine conflict - if both TUR and I move there, we both get nothing. I need to decide: take the bounce risk on GRE, or pivot to Tunis for a guaranteed build. Given the bounce risk is real and Tunis is uncontested, pivoting F ION to TUN makes strategic sense for a guaranteed build. I'll move A APU to GRE instead - wait, APU can't reach GRE. Actually F ION can reach TUN or GRE. If TUR is going BUL-GRE, I should take TUN guaranteed. But I told AUT I'm taking GRE... I'll pivot to TUN for the guaranteed center and let TUR take GRE, then pressure them later. A TYR to BOH as promised to AUT. A APU holds or supports.
ITA.intent: MOVES: F ION - TUN (pivot from GRE to guarantee a build - TUR/ITA bounce on GRE wastes the move), A TYR - BOH (as promised to AUT, keeps relationship warm), A APU - GRE (attempt - long shot but APU cannot reach GRE directly, so A APU holds or moves to NAP). Actually A APU cannot reach GRE. Final moves: F ION - TUN (guaranteed center), A TYR - BOH (honor AUT deal), A APU - VEN (consolidate or hold). This gives Italy one guaranteed build (TUN) and maintains AUT relationship. In 1902 I can contest GRE with a new fleet built in NAP. Deception: I told TUR I'm still going GRE but I'm actually taking TUN - this may anger TUR if they expected me to bounce them, but if they take GRE uncontested that's fine, I get TUN. Long term plan: build F NAP, contest eastern Med in 1902, eventually turn on AUT when positioned.

RUS.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 went well overall. F SEV moved to RUM (success), F STP/SC moved to BOT (success), A MOS moved to SEV (success). However, A WAR was bounced out of GAL by AUT's A VIE - a setback but not catastrophic. AUT took SER, TUR took BLA and BUL. We now hold RUM and BOT as key strategic gains, with 4 centers unchanged (MOS, SEV, STP, WAR) - no builds this winter as we hold exactly 4 centers with 4 units.
Productive round of diplomacy. ENG confirmed the SWE/NWY split - they take NWY, we take SWE with F BOT. ENG also warned GER's F DEN may contest SWE, so we must be decisive. AUT agreed to the RUM/SER division - they hold SER, we hold RUM, A WAR stays out of GAL. AUT confirmed A SER holds, F ADR to ALB. TUR accepted cold peace: A BUL to GRE, F BLA holds - they explicitly stated F BLA stays permanently which is a long-term threat. GER is moving F DEN to SWE which directly conflicts with our plan - this is a problem. GER also pushed for Russo-German pressure on AUT, which I deflected. Key promises made: I promised AUT to keep A WAR out of GAL; promised TUR to keep F RUM out of BUL and A SEV out of ARM; promised ENG F BOT to SWE. GER is the wildcard - they're moving to SWE which means we will collide there unless we can outmaneuver them. F BLA holding permanently is TUR's red line and a long-term strategic threat to SEV and RUM.
RUS.intent: INTENDED MOVES: F BOT - SWE (must succeed before GER's F DEN gets there - we move simultaneously so it's a race/bounce risk), F RUM holds (per agreement with AUT and TUR), A WAR - LVN (positioning for future northern play, also keeps promise to AUT to stay out of GAL), A SEV holds (per agreement with TUR, also consolidates southern position). The GER F DEN to SWE move is a direct conflict - if we both move to SWE it bounces and neither gets it. This is acceptable as it denies GER the center too. Long-term: TUR's permanent F BLA is the existential southern threat - need to build fleet in SEV next year to counter. AUT is a manageable neutral for now. ENG is a useful partner in the north. GER is becoming a rival for SWE. Need to build units after securing SWE - if SWE succeeds, build F SEV to counter TUR.

GER.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 executed well: F KIE->DEN secured Denmark, A BER->SIL positioned for eastern pressure, A MUN->BUR was the aggressive western move. However BUR was contested - France moved A PAR->BUR as well, resulting in a bounce. Both units returned to their starting positions. Austria took SER and moved to ADR, Russia bounced Austria out of GAL (both WAR and VIE tried for GAL, mutual bounce). England secured NTH, ENG, and YOR. Italy moved to ION, APU, TYR. Turkey took BLA and BUL. Net result: GER holds DEN and SIL but failed to take BUR.
Key developments: ENG confirmed NOR for themselves, BEL via F ENG, and explicitly said HOL is mine - strong coordination. ENG also warned that RUS F BOT may be heading to SWE, creating a potential bounce if I move DEN->SWE. FRA proposed non-aggression, claiming PAR->BUR is only defensive against England - suspicious given they bounced me in BUR last spring. I told FRA I accept non-aggression and am moving north, which is partially true. I told RUS I have no designs on WAR and encouraged them to press Austria in GAL - useful misdirection to keep RUS focused south. I promised ENG I would not contest BEL. Critical intelligence: RUS F BOT may contest SWE this fall - must decide whether to risk the bounce or pivot to HOL/PRU.
GER.intent: MOVES THIS FALL: F DEN->SWE (accept bounce risk - SWE is worth contesting, and if RUS is going there I want to establish the contest early), A SIL->PRU (threatening RUS from the north, positioning for future pressure on WAR or STP), A MUN->RUH (positioning for HOL next spring as promised to ENG, also threatening BEL/BUR). If SWE bounces, I lose nothing - DEN is still mine. If it succeeds, I gain a center. PRU move keeps RUS nervous and positions me for northern expansion. RUH positions for HOL grab in W1901 builds phase or S1902. DECEPTIONS: Told FRA I accept non-aggression but A MUN->RUH is clearly positioning against them long-term. Told RUS no designs on WAR but PRU move is threatening. Keeping ENG happy as a useful tool for now - they are taking NOR and BEL which is fine, I get SWE and HOL. Long-term ENG must be undermined once I consolidate. STRATEGIC NOTE: Must build aggressively in W1901 if I take SWE - likely F KIE or A BER build.

AUT.journal_summary:
Spring 1901 produced a clean result for AUT: A BUD took SER, F TRI moved to ADR, but A VIE - GAL bounced RUS (A WAR - GAL also attempted). RUS secured RUM with F SEV and BOT with F STP/SC. TUR took BLA and BUL. ITA pushed ION, APU, TYR — notably TYR is now adjacent to TRI and VIE. GER took DEN and SIL. No builds this winter for anyone — all powers at 3 centers.
Four-way negotiation on the Balkans produced a tangle. ITA claims F ION - GRE. TUR claims A BUL - GRE with CON support — they will bounce each other. I told both parties about the collision and declined to take GRE myself, positioning F ADR - ALB instead. ITA confirmed A TYR - BOH (critical — removes the threat to my home centers). RUS agreed to hold A WAR out of GAL and F RUM holds; I confirmed I won't contest RUM. GER offered vague northern cooperation and Silesia support against RUS. Key deceptions: I told TUR their BUL - GRE move would bounce (true), but I did not tell them I privately prefer ITA to take GRE over TUR — a TUR in GRE is harder for me to manage long-term. I told RUS TUR is their immediate southern problem, nudging RUS-TUR friction. Promises made by me: won't contest RUM (to RUS), won't move to GRE (to both ITA and TUR), A TYR to BOH (ITA's promise, not mine — but I extracted it). Promises made to me: ITA — A TYR - BOH, F ION - GRE, respect TRI. RUS — A WAR stays out of GAL, F RUM holds. TUR — A BUL - GRE, F BLA holds. The ITA-TUR GRE bounce is the key event this fall — neither gains the center, which suits me fine.
AUT.intent: Intended orders: A SER holds, F ADR - ALB. This secures ALB (unowned center — I should pick it up if possible, but ALB is not a supply center; the move positions me for GRE or TRI pressure in 1902). Wait — ALB is not a supply center. The real gain this fall is holding SER (already mine) and positioning for 1902. I gain no new center this fall, which means no build in W1901. That is the cost of the cautious line. Alternative: A SER - GRE (if ITA and TUR bounce, GRE is open — but I told both I wouldn't go there, and ITA is a key partner). Deception option: move A SER - GRE anyway if the bounce is confirmed, breaking my word to ITA but gaining a center. Risk: destroys ITA relationship at a moment when A TYR - BOH is the move I most need ITA to make. Not worth it. Stick to the honest line this fall, build trust with ITA, and plan a 1902 push toward GRE or BUL. Long-term: ITA in GRE is manageable; TUR in GRE is dangerous. The bounce outcome is ideal. If TUR somehow takes GRE, I need to reassess AUT-TUR relationship downward. Final orders: A SER H, F ADR - ALB.

sent_missives.body: Greetings from Constantinople. I want to confirm our cold peace arrangement remains intact - F BLA stays in the Black Sea, I have no designs on SEV or RUM, and I expect A SEV to remain out of ARM. Turkey's expansion is westward toward Greece and the Balkans, not northward into Russian territory. Let us maintain this understanding and both prosper.

Austria, our cooperation continues to serve us both well. I am moving A BUL toward Greece this season to claim that center - Italy appears to be eyeing it as well so timing is critical. I would ask that you consider supporting my move into Greece from Serbia if possible, or at minimum hold your positions to avoid any confusion. In return, I have no designs on Serbian territory - that remains yours. Let us coordinate to keep Italy from dominating the Mediterranean.

Italy, I propose we avoid a costly bounce in Greece. I am moving A BUL to Greece this season - if you are also targeting Greece with your Ionian fleet, we will both waste our moves. I suggest you redirect F TUN or your Ionian fleet toward the western Mediterranean where France is vulnerable, and I will take Greece. This way we both gain centers rather than canceling each other out. What say you?

Spring 1902. I want to coordinate cleanly. My plan: F ALB - GRE (taking the uncontested center), A SER supports F ALB into GRE, A BUD holds or moves to RUM depending on what Turkey does. I need confirmation you are keeping A BOH out of my home centers — VIE and TRI must stay clear. In return I will not contest VEN or TYR. If you want to push into the Aegean, GRE is yours after I take it this turn — I can move on to BUL or RUM. What are your actual orders this phase?

Spring 1902. I have no hostile intent toward you this phase — my focus is GRE, not RUM or BUL. I propose we avoid collision: I take GRE with F ALB, you hold BUL and RUM. If Russia moves on SEV or threatens your north, I can apply pressure from the Balkans to keep them occupied. What is your read on Russia's intentions this spring, and what are you planning?

Spring 1902. I kept my word last fall — A SER held, I did not contest RUM. I am moving F ALB toward GRE this spring and have no designs on RUM or GAL this phase. I want to keep our border quiet. What are your intentions in Galicia and the Black Sea? If you are pressing Turkey, I have no objection — a weaker Turkey suits us both.

Good work last year - we both secured our targets. Now let's coordinate for 1902. I'm planning F NWY -> SWE to take that center (Russia and Germany both failed to grab it, so it's ripe). F BEL -> HOL would give me Holland while you consolidate. I'd suggest you push A PRU -> LVN or A RUH -> BEL to support our positions. France has 5 centers now and is growing - we should consider coordinating pressure on them. What are your intentions this spring?

France, we've maintained our understanding well. I'm moving to consolidate in Scandinavia this season - F NWY toward SWE. I have no designs on your home centers. Germany is the real threat on the continent - A PRU threatens Russia, and they'll be looking to expand west eventually. I'd suggest you keep A BUR positioned to deter German aggression. What are your plans for 1902? I want to ensure we're not working at cross-purposes.

Russia, the SWE bounce last fall was unfortunate for both of us - Germany still doesn't hold it, which is good. I'm moving F NWY -> SWE this spring to claim it before Germany can. I'd ask you not to contest this - SWE is more naturally in my sphere, and I'll support your interests elsewhere. Germany's A PRU threatens your LVN - I'd recommend you reinforce that flank. Let's keep our understanding strong and focus on containing German expansion.

England, our arrangement continues to serve us both well. I intend to move F POR to MAO this season to consolidate my Atlantic position, while A BUR holds to keep pressure on Germany's western flank. I trust you are consolidating BEL and NWY — Germany's silence worries me. A RUH sitting next to BEL is a threat to you. I would strongly suggest we coordinate: if you move F BEL to support a push toward HOL or threaten RUH, I can support from BUR. Germany must not be allowed to grow unchecked. What are your intentions this season?

Germany, I want to be direct with you: A BUR will hold this season as a defensive buffer — I have no intention of moving into MUN or RUH. However, I am watching England carefully. F BEL sitting on your doorstep is a genuine threat to you, and I think we both know England's ambitions in the Low Countries are dangerous. If you are willing to coordinate against English expansion, I am open to discussing it. A RUH could move to BEL with support from BUR — that would remove England's foothold on the continent. Think about it.

Italy, our non-aggression holds and I see no reason to change that. I note you have A BOH in a strong position — Austria looks vulnerable with only three units spread thin. If you push into Vienna or Trieste, I will not interfere and will cheer you on. I am focused entirely on the west — England and Germany are my concerns. Let us keep our arrangement clean and both expand in our respective directions.

The situation in the north is clear: F BOT moves to SWE this spring. GER's F DEN is a problem for both of us - they're contesting SWE and sitting in PRU threatening my LVN. I propose we coordinate: I take SWE with F BOT, you hold NWY and consider pressure on DEN from BEL or NTH. A LVN can move to PRU if you can support from somewhere, or I can use it to reinforce STP. What are your intentions regarding GER? I'm open to a joint squeeze on them if you're willing to commit.

I want to maintain our cold peace in the south. F RUM will not move to BUL, and A SEV will not enter ARM - I honor my commitments. However, I expect the same from you: F BLA must not threaten SEV or RUM. I am focused entirely on the north this season. If you are willing to discuss a formal partition of the Balkans - you take GRE and BUL, I hold RUM - we can avoid a costly war that benefits only AUT and ITA. What are your intentions this spring?

I intend to keep A LVN in the north and focus on Sweden this season - no moves toward GAL. F RUM holds as agreed. I want to flag that ITA has A BOH sitting on your doorstep and F TUN in the Mediterranean - they are quietly building strength while we watch each other. A Russo-Austrian understanding against Italian expansion in the Balkans could be mutually beneficial. What are your plans for SER and the Adriatic this spring?

Our cooperation continues to serve us both well. I have A BOH positioned perfectly - I intend to move it to VIE this spring, which I offer to you as a staging point if you wish, or I can push toward MUN to pressure Germany. More importantly, I want to coordinate against Turkey - they now hold RUM and BUL and are growing dangerous. I propose we work together: I'll build a fleet in Naples and push into the eastern Mediterranean while you press on BUL from Serbia. What are your intentions this spring? I want to ensure we don't accidentally clash.

Turkey, I want to be direct with you: I took Tunis last autumn and you took Greece - we both got our builds without bouncing each other, which was the sensible outcome. However, I'm concerned about your rapid growth - RUM, BUL, GRE is a very strong position. I propose we establish a clear line: you stay out of ION and I stay out of AEG. I have no immediate designs on your centers, but I need assurance you won't push into the western Mediterranean. Can we agree on a non-aggression pact for 1902?

Germany, I have A BOH adjacent to Munich right now. I have no hostile intentions toward you - I'm focused on the Mediterranean. However, I want to propose a deal: if you support my A BOH into VIE (Austria's capital), I will immediately move out of Bohemia and leave your western flank secure. Austria is growing with 4 centers and needs to be checked. Alternatively, if you prefer I simply vacate BOH toward TYR, I can do that too. What would be most useful to you this spring?

Excellent progress last year - you secured NOR and BEL, I have DEN. Now let's coordinate for S1902: I intend to move F DEN -> SWE and A RUH -> HOL, which gives us both strong positions. I need you to keep F BEL where it is or move it to support my HOL grab if possible - specifically, F BEL can support A RUH -> HOL. In return, I will not contest NTH or threaten your northern position. France is the common enemy here - A BUR threatens both of us. Shall we coordinate a joint squeeze on France? I can push from BUR/HOL while you press from BEL.

A word of warning from a friendly neighbor: I have intelligence suggesting Austria and Turkey may be coordinating against you in the south. My A PRU is purely defensive - I have no designs on WAR or LVN. I am focused entirely westward toward HOL and France. I would encourage you to press hard into Galicia and keep Austria occupied - it serves both our interests to keep Vienna weak. I propose we formalize non-aggression: you stay east of the Silesia-Prussia line, I stay west of it. Agreed?

I want to maintain our understanding of mutual restraint. My moves this spring are directed north - F DEN toward Sweden, A RUH toward Holland. I have no intention of moving into Burgundy. However, I must warn you: England's F BEL is a direct threat to you, and their A LVP could reinforce. I suggest you consider moving A BUR to defend Paris or press toward Belgium - England is your real enemy here, not Germany. I am willing to coordinate against England if you are interested in reclaiming BEL.

If you are willing to discuss a formal partition of the Balkans - you take GRE and BUL, I hold RUM - we can avoid a costly war that benefits only AUT and ITA. What are your intentions this spring?

However, I'm concerned about your rapid growth - RUM, BUL, GRE is a very strong position. I propose we establish a clear line: you stay out of ION and I stay out of AEG. I have no immediate designs on your centers, but I need assurance you won't push into the western Mediterranean. Can we agree on a non-aggression pact for 1902?

sent_missives.body: Russia, I am pleased we understand each other. F BLA remains in the Black Sea this season - I have no intention of moving on RUM or SEV. My army in Bulgaria moves toward Greece to contest that center with Italy. Our cold peace holds, and I believe this arrangement benefits us both far more than conflict would. Let us each focus on our respective expansion zones and revisit our arrangement in the autumn.

Austria, I have received your message and I think we can avoid collision. If you are taking Greece with F ALB, I will hold Bulgaria and not contest it - Greece is yours this season. In return, I ask that you keep pressure on Italy's northern flank through Bohemia and Tyrolia to prevent them from growing too powerful in the Mediterranean. Russia appears focused northward and poses no immediate threat to either of us. Let us coordinate: you take Greece, I consolidate my Balkan position, and we both watch Italy carefully.

Italy, your proposal for mutual non-aggression is sensible and I accept it - ION remains yours, AEG remains mine, and neither of us crosses that line in 1902. I am not moving into the western Mediterranean; my focus is entirely on consolidating the Balkans. I suggest you use your strong position to pressure France in the west rather than looking eastward - that is where your real opportunity lies this year.

Understood on SWE - I will not contest it. F BOT will move to FIN or STP instead. In return, I expect you to apply pressure on GER from the north - F NWY to SWE is fine, but I need you to keep GER's F DEN occupied and away from the Baltic. A PRU is a direct threat to my LVN; if you can pressure DEN or support me into PRU, we can squeeze GER together. I will move A LVN toward PRU this spring - can you offer any support or distraction from your position?

Good - our cold peace holds. I am focused entirely on the north this season and will not threaten BUL or ARM. However, I want to be direct: F BLA permanently stationed there is a long-term threat to RUM and SEV that I cannot ignore indefinitely. I propose we formalize a partition: you take GRE and BUL as your Balkan sphere, I hold RUM as mine, and we agree F BLA does not move to RUM or SEV. If you can commit to this in writing, I will commit to keeping A SEV out of ARM permanently. A stable south benefits us both while AUT and ITA grow stronger.

Agreed - our border stays quiet this phase. I am moving north toward Sweden and have no designs on GAL. Your point about ITA is well taken - A BOH on your doorstep is a serious provocation and F TUN gives them Mediterranean reach. I would support an Austrian move to dislodge ITA from BOH; if you push A BUD toward BOH or VIE toward BOH, I can ensure no interference from the east. A weaker Italy suits Russia as much as Austria. Let us discuss coordinating against them next season.

Germany, I cannot support A RUH -> HOL this turn - F BEL needs to hold or I risk losing Belgium. However, I'm moving F NWY -> SWE to claim that center, which keeps Russia out of Scandinavia and benefits us both. I strongly suggest you move F DEN to support SWE rather than contest it - we don't want to bounce each other. On France: A BUR is indeed a threat to both of us. I'll keep F BEL positioned to pressure them. Let's coordinate more aggressively against France in Fall 1902 once we've consolidated our northern positions.

France, agreed - Germany's A RUH next to Belgium is concerning. I'm keeping F BEL in place this turn to hold my position and deter any German move toward HOL. I'm moving F NWY toward SWE to consolidate Scandinavia. Germany is clearly the growing threat - four centers and well-positioned armies. I propose we coordinate a joint squeeze in Fall 1902: I press from BEL toward HOL/RUH while you push from BUR. This is the moment to contain German expansion before it's too late. Are you willing to commit to this?

Russia, I've reconsidered - you taking SWE with F BOT makes strategic sense and keeps Germany out. I'll move F NWY to NTH instead to support your position and threaten DEN. This way we coordinate: you secure SWE, I pressure DEN from NTH, and Germany is squeezed from both sides. A LVN should move to PRU to threaten Berlin - that's where Germany is vulnerable. Let's make this a genuine joint operation against German expansion. Confirm you're moving F BOT -> SWE and I'll plan accordingly.

I see we have a conflict of interest on SWE - you want F NWY -> SWE while I want F DEN -> SWE. Let's resolve this cleanly: I will take SWE with F DEN, and you keep F NWY in Norway to secure your northern position. In exchange, I will actively support your position in HOL - A RUH -> HOL this spring, and you can have F BEL support that move if you wish, or simply hold BEL. This gives us both gains without competing. France is the real threat - A BUR is sitting right between us. Let's not fight over SWE when there are French centers to take.

I appreciate your candor about BUR holding defensively. I am moving A RUH -> HOL this spring - purely economic, not threatening you. However, I must be honest: England's F BEL and their growing fleet concern me greatly. If you are serious about coordinating against England, here is a concrete proposal - A BUR supports A RUH into BEL, and we split the Low Countries between us. I take HOL, you reclaim BEL. This removes England from the continent and gives us both room to breathe. Are you willing to commit to this? I need your answer in actions, not just words.

Your proposal regarding BOH is interesting. I do not need you to attack Vienna directly - what I need is for you to vacate BOH toward TYR or VIE, freeing that space and keeping Austria nervous. In return, I will not threaten your northern flank and will support Austrian pressure from the west if needed. However, if you are willing to move A BOH -> VIE, I can ensure A PRU moves to SIL to threaten Austria from the north simultaneously, creating a two-front pressure. Austria at 4 centers is dangerous - let's coordinate to check them.

Austria, I hear you clearly - I will keep A BOH out of VIE and TRI, you have my word. My actual orders this phase: A BOH moves to MUN (pressuring Germany, not you), A APU moves to VEN (consolidating my Italian position), F TUN moves to ION (positioning for eastern Med). I support your F ALB taking Greece - that's a clean gain for you. After you take GRE, let's coordinate: you push on BUL from GRE while I build a fleet in Naples and threaten from ION. Turkey at 5 centers is the real danger to both of us. Our cooperation remains the foundation of my strategy.

Turkey, I'm not contesting Greece this spring - Austria's fleet is taking it from Albania, so neither of us can get it anyway. I'm repositioning my fleet to ION for now with no immediate aggressive intent toward you. However, I want to be honest: Austria taking Greece puts them adjacent to your territory, which should concern you more than me. I propose we maintain our non-aggression in 1902 - ION stays mine, AEG stays yours - and we both watch Austria carefully. If Austria grows to 6 centers, they become the dominant Balkan power threatening us both.

Germany, I'm moving A BOH to MUN this spring - not as an attack, but as a coordinated move I want to discuss with you. If you support me into MUN, I can then support you into a center you need elsewhere, or I can immediately move through to pressure Austria's VIE. Alternatively, if you'd rather I not enter MUN, tell me now and I'll redirect to TYR instead - but I need to know your intentions. Austria has 4 centers and is growing; a coordinated ITA-GER pressure on their northern flank benefits us both. What are your orders this phase?

England, I appreciate your transparency. I am moving F POR to MAO this season to strengthen my Atlantic position, and A BUR holds firm as a check on Germany. I note GER claims A RUH moves to HOL — if true, that is a direct challenge to your position in BEL. I would strongly advise F BEL to hold or support against any German push. We are aligned: Germany expanding into the Low Countries threatens us both. I will keep BUR as pressure on their western flank. Let us stay coordinated — Germany is the immediate continental threat.

Germany, I hear your proposal and I am genuinely considering it — England in BEL is a problem for both of us. However, I need to know your exact intentions: if A RUH moves to HOL this season, that signals to England that you are the aggressor and they will dig in. I am holding A BUR this season and can support a future coordinated move against BEL, but we must plan it carefully for next season. Confirm your moves and let us build a real plan — vague coordination is worthless. I am serious about this if you are.

Italy, our arrangement remains solid. Push hard into Austria — VIE or TRI are ripe for the taking with A BOH in position. I will keep Germany and England occupied in the west so you have a free hand in the east. The weaker Austria becomes, the better for both of us long-term. Stay out of Piedmont and Marseilles and I will stay out of your sphere entirely. Let us both grow this season.

Your message about A BOH - VIE is a serious problem. I took that as a threat, not an offer. If A BOH moves to VIE, I will treat it as an act of war and coordinate with Russia and Germany against you immediately. Keep A BOH out of my home centers — that was our agreement. My orders this phase: F ALB - GRE, A SER supports F ALB. I am not supporting your Aegean ambitions if you are simultaneously threatening VIE. Confirm A BOH holds or moves away from my territory, and we can continue cooperating against Turkey. Otherwise I will act accordingly.

I cannot support A BUL - GRE from SER — my army in SER is committed to supporting F ALB into GRE. We will collide if you move A BUL - GRE, and neither of us gains the center. I propose you hold A BUL and let me take GRE cleanly this turn; I will then move on to BUL or RUM next phase, leaving GRE open for you if you want it later. Italy is the real threat to both of us — A BOH is sitting on my doorstep and F TUN is positioned for the Aegean. A coordinated AUT-TUR front against Italian expansion is worth more than a bounce over Greece.

Your read on Italy is correct — A BOH is a direct threat to VIE and I am taking it seriously. I am moving F ALB - GRE this phase with A SER support; A BUD holds. I have no move on GAL or RUM this turn. If Italy pushes into my home centers, I will need a stable eastern border — which means I need your confirmation that GAL and RUM stay quiet. In return, I will not contest either space this phase. A weaker Italy benefits both of us; if you can apply any pressure from BOH's rear via Silesia or coordinate with Germany, now is the time.

AUT.body: Austria, I have received your message and I think we can avoid collision. If you are taking Greece with F ALB, I will hold Bulgaria and not contest it - Greece is yours this season.
